description: A hook-like arrow attached to a strong rope shoots for 20 metres, attaching itself to the target. It allows the shooter to pull the hunted beast towards them.
name: Hunting Harpoon (Single)
notes: Usually reserved for fishing large sea beasts.
usage:
Heavy: 
Subtract 1d10 from the Attack roll for every point below 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 1 in Athletics and 3 in Brute Force would have a 2d10 penalty on the Range Attack)
Add 1d10 for every point above 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 3 Athletics and 4 Brute Force would have a 1d10 bonus on a Range Attack with the harpoon)

Retractable: If the harpoon hits its target, the Attacker can use an action to attempt to pull the target towards them (the target can do an Athletics OR Brute Force plus Agility to disentangle themselves: the number of successes must be equal or higher than the difference between the previous Attack minus Defence roll). If the target is a heavy surface, like a porous rock, the target can roll Athletics + Acrobatics to be pulled towards it.

description: Two hook-like arrows attached to a strong rope shoots for 20 metres, attaching themselves to the target. It allows the shooter to pull the hunted beast towards them.
name: Hunting Harpoon (Dual Shot)
notes: A twist on the regular harpoon for faster shooting. The dual-shot version allows the shooter to use a Reaction to shoot again as part of the main attack.
usage:
Heavy: 
Subtract 2d10 from the Attack roll for every point below 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 1 in Athletics and 3 in Brute Force would have a 4d10 penalty on the Range Attack)
Add 2d10 for every point above 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 4 Athletics and 4 Brute Force would have a 4d10 bonus on a Range Attack with the harpoon)

Retractable: If the harpoon hits its target, the Attacker can use an action to attempt to pull the target towards them (the target can do an Athletics OR Brute Force plus Agility to disentangle themselves: the number of successes must be equal or higher than the number of Successes from the Attack roll). If the target is a heavy surface, like a porous rock, the target can roll Athletics + Acrobatics to be pulled towards it.

description: The flamethrower spits flames in a 15-metre cone, incinerating everything in its path.
name: Flame Thrower
notes: Use of the flame thrower is illegal in Neblir. Its military use has been limited to Buffer expeditions, and even then heavily criticised by sentient rights movements.
usage:
Pulling the trigger on the Flame Thrower creates a cone of fire from the shooter: 15m long and up to 2m wide. Anyone within the cone area can use a Reaction to make an Agility + Acrobatics save to take half damage. Additionally, any targets who fail the save, are set on fire for 5 rounds, taking 1d4 every round on their turn unless they put the fire out.

Heavy: 
Subtract 1d10 from the Attack roll for every point below 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 1 in Athletics and 3 in Brute Force would have a 2d10 penalty on the Range Attack)
Add 1d10 for every point above 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 3 Athletics and 4 Brute Force would have a 1d10 bonus on a Range Attack with the harpoon).

costInfo: The cost includes the injector. A cartridge to be used with the injector or an injection gun costs 100 CONF.
description: An injector loaded with a paralysing substance. The attacker must inject the target but the effect is immediate as the target takes the full effect of the drug.
name: Paralyse Shot
usage: The attacker must make a Melee Attack against the target. On a Success, the target makes a Resistance (Internal) save against any successes from the damage roll. If there are any outstanding successes, the victim is paralysed for a number of minutes equal to the outstanding successes.

costInfo: The cost includes the injector. A cartridge to be used with the injector or an injection gun costs 100 CONF.
description: An injector loaded with a sleep-inducing substance. The attacker must inject the target but the effect is immediate as the target takes the full effect of the drug.
name: Sleep Shot
usage: The attacker must make a Melee Attack against the target. On a Success, the target makes a Resistance (Internal) save against any successes from the damage roll. If there are any outstanding successes, the victim falls asleep for a number of hours equal to the outstanding successes.

costInfo: The cost includes the injector. A cartridge to be used with the injector or an injection gun costs 100 CONF.
description: An injector loaded with a life-draining substance. The attacker must inject the target but the effect is immediate as the target takes the full effect of the drug.
name: Death Shot
usage: The victim makes a Resistance (Internal) save against any successes from the damage roll. On a failure, it takes 5d6 damage. On a success, the damage rolled is halved.

description: An oval-shaped flask made of modified glass contains a strong sleeping agent. The attacker clicks the lid, which changes the molecular composition of the glass so that it shatters more easily upon impact. The attacker then lobs the Sleep Bomb in the desired direction, where it shatters, creating a 20-metre red vapour cloud, inducing those inside it to sleep.
name: Sleep Bomb
notes:
Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage:
Smash it on the floor or throw (Athletics + Aim roll).

Anyone in the affected area must make a Resistance (Internal) save. If the successes rolled by the Sleep Bomb damage (5d10) are higher than the Resistance (Internal) save, the victim falls asleep.

description: A gun crafted to shoot cartridges containing injectable substances, often used by security teams not wanting to use lethal force.
name: Injection Gun
notes: Can be used to shoot injectable substances, such as paralysing and sleeping agents.
usage:
By default, the gun takes 6 cartridges. It takes 1 Action to load one cartridge into the gun, or to swap cartriges.

Check the substance in the sho cartridge for its effects, noting that the number of damage dice is halved. For instance, if shooting a sleeping agent that would normally roll 10d10 when injected directly, it is 5d10 when shot with the injection gun. The target, if hit, would have to make a Resistance (Internal) check against 5d10, not 10d10.

costInfo: This item is not for sale.
description:
Tiara-like, powered by Siike at an unprecedented Ikum concentration, somehow incremented to a looping point by a chain of crystallised Siike nodes, contained by an extremely intricate subtle circuit of anti-Siike energy*. The device is placed as a crown on the wearer, connecting to their GRID and directly to their brain via quantum entanglement.
The wearer can ask a question to their future self. The complexity of the question and how far into the future it is asked will determine the potency of the quantum entanglement required and the potential harm the user suffers.
name: Quantum Oracle
notes:
Experimental NF Fenne tech. Its existence remains disputed by most scholars. Found in a basement full of discarded experiments and other tech, purchased in the black market by Fabulous Krek, thinking his scientists could make sense of it. In 8 of 2096, NIN agent Della Whyt returned the Northern Crown, or Quantum Oracle, to the Northern Federation.
*As of 2096, anti-Siike energy is a conceptual matter, a theoretical possibility, not proven science.
usage:
If locked: Deduction + Mechanics to unlock it (3 Successes needed if it’s the first time, 2 if it’s the second time, 1 from then on).
If unlocked, upon connecting it to the user’s GRID, the following warning is displayed:
“WARNING: Operation of the Quantum Oracle is exceedingly perilous. Great care is advised when considering use. This device is currently intended for experimental use only. If you have not been trained specifically, you must  immediately cease experimentation. Danger of death. Danger of severe mental impairment. Even if qualified directly under Dr Syna Urv, never, under any circumstances, should the user operate the Quantum Oracle alone.”
One Action to place the QO on one’s head and wait for it to connect to the brain of the GRID-connected user.
The QO must have access to a high-Siike charging port. If not, the following message is displayed:
“The Quantum Oracle requires a high-power Siike charging port. High-power Siike charging ports are subject to the most strict safety standards. Ensure all certificates are up to date and all relevant safety checks have passed successfully before proceeding.”
If connected via GRID and a high-energy Siike source, the following warning appears in the wearer’s GRID upon placing the Quantum Oracle on their heads:
“Only operate in a safe environment surrounded by a professional qualified under Dr Syna Urv.
You must have passed the relevant training before operating the Quantum Oracle.
Do you wish to proceed?”
If they do:
“Upon integration with the Quantum Oracle, you won’t be able to interact with those around you until you disconnect. Remember they won’t be able to interact with you either until you disconnect.
Do you wish to integrate?”
If they integrate:
The user must make a Mentality + GRID + GRID Mod check (3 Successes necessary to connect successfully):
On a failure, the user takes 1d6 Mental damage as thoughts, ideas and memories are scrambled, and the QO becomes locked.
On a success: continue.
If successfully integrated with a user’s brain:
Your vision blurs, then fades to black. You lose all sensation. Nothing hurts. You don’t feel anything. Perhaps because nothing is. But you are. You are nothing and everything that is possible at the same time. You exist, you are aware of your existence, but it’s like your consciousness has been disconnected and floats in a sea of where the known and the unknown coalesce with the possible and the impossible. Everything is dark, everything is silent, everything is before you and nothing is. And then a voice or is it text or is it a thought…
“Congratulations, you have connected to a different version of you. You are not quite here nor there, not quite now nor then. This is your quantum self.
Using this heightened state of being, you can send a question into the future, to be answered by you, a future you, one that may know the answer.
You must understand this poses a high risk to your mental wellbeing. Consider how you think you would react when asked a certain question. Consider you may receive no answer; this could be because you can’t or won’t answer, or because you no longer exist in the time you have set the question to be asked at.
Thus, before proceeding, some advice:
Limit the complexity of your questions and narrow it down as much as possible; thus, multiple-answer questions are preferable to open questions but less so than binary questions.
Limit the time frame: after all, you are more likely to be alive in an hour than ten years from now.
Finally, avoid paradoxical questions, such as ‘What am I going to ask next?’ These have proven particularly lethal in previous tests due to their propensity to cause quantum loops, which are particularly difficult to disconnect from.
Once you have worded your question, you must think of asking it. The Quantum Oracle will let you know it has been asked.
If you do not wish to proceed, you must think of disconnection. Remember, those outside cannot interact with you right now, for you are in a state too complex to be constrained by time and space.
You can think of disconnecting at any moment, but it is best practice to wait for an answer if a question has already been asked. Otherwise, you may continue to ask questions, but please remember the risks stated.
Congratulations once again. You have become your very own Quantum Oracle.”
One Action to disconnect: roll Mentality + GRID + GRID Mod (2 Successes needed); on a failure, take 1d6 Mental damage (2d6 if disconnecting before waiting for an answer). You can try again or continue asking questions.
One Action to ask a question and set the time towards which it is projected. Resolve the question, take damage if necessary, then decide whether to continue asking, or disconnect.

description: A Siike powered explosive device that allows the user to alter the nature of the Siike composition.
name: Siike Explosive (small)
usage:
1 Action to activate: triggered by the user, a timer or movement sensor (10m radius).

If set to explode immediately, it does so at the end of the user's next turn.

1 Action to alter composition: increasing Sinum and reducing Ette makes the explosive more volatile.

Common settings:

High: 12d6 within a 10m radius. (Make a Deduction + Science check; on a failure, roll 1d10, on a 5 or lower, it explodes; one success makes the device explode as programmed, plus add 1d6 damage for each success as you adjust the composition for maximum efficacy).

Medium: 10d6 (Make a Deduction + Science check; on a failure, subtract 2d6 from the damage; on a success, add 1d6 per success).

Low: 6d6 damage guaranteed.

The Siike device has a range of a 10m radius. The damage is halved outside this radius, up to a 20m radius. No damage outside the 20m radius. In addition, any character within either radius can make an Agility + Acrobatics save to halve the damage.

description: A Siike powered explosive device that allows the user to alter the nature of the Siike composition.
name: Siike Explosive (large)
usage:
1 Action to activate: triggered by the user, a timer or movement sensor (20m radius).

If set to explode immediately, it does so at the end of the user's next turn.

1 Action to alter composition: increasing Sinum and reducing Ette makes the explosive more volatile.

Common settings:

High: 24d6 within a 20m radius. (Make a Deduction + Science check; on a failure, roll 1d10, on a 5 or lower, it explodes; one success makes the device explode as programmed, plus add 1d6 damage for each success as you adjust the composition for maximum efficacy).

Medium: 20d6 (Make a Deduction + Science check; on a failure, subtract 2d6 from the damage; on a success, add 1d6 per success).

Low: 12d6 damage guaranteed.

The Siike device has a range of a 20m radius. The damage is halved outside this radius, up to a 40m radius. No damage outside the 40m radius. In addition, any character within either radius can make an Agility + Acrobatics save to halve the damage.

costInfo: They can often be bought as a set with Magnetic Gloves for 18000 CONF
description: These smart magnetic soles can be added to existing footwear. The soles can adapt to a surface's magnetic composition to facilitate walking on walls or even ceilings. They work best in combination with Magnetic Gloves.
name: Magnetic Soles
notes:
The soles can theoretically be clipped to Siike Boots, but discharging Siike while wearing the soles has a 25% chance of breaking the magnetic soles (roll 1d100, on a 25 or lower, the soles break).

If used in conjunction with the Magnetic Gloves, it takes 1 Action to put on each pair.
usage:
It takes 1 Action to put them on.

The wearer can add a 2d10 per pair on any Athletics + Acrobatics to climb up walls and ceilings.

The soles are clipped onto existing footwear.

The Speed when climbing is 25% of the wearer’s Speed or 50% if wearing both gloves and soles.

The soles have 5 uses, each giving the character 1 hour.

costInfo: They can often be bought as a set with Magnetic Soles for 18000 CONF
description: These smart magnetic gloves can adapt to a surface's magnetic composition to facilitate walking on walls or even ceilings. They work best in combination with Magnetic Soles.
name: Magnetic Gloves
notes: If used in conjunction with the Magnetic Soles, it takes 1 Action to put on each pair.
usage:
The gloves are chunky and impose a 4d10 penalty on any Manual-based checks (minimum 1 die).

The Speed when climbing is 25% of the wearer’s Speed or 50% if wearing both gloves and soles.

The gloves have 5 uses, each giving the character 1 hour.

description: The Siike boots are imbued with Siike energy that bursts when triggered by the wearer’s GRID. They propel the wearer in the direction of their choosing.
name: Siike Boots (Regular)
usage:
The Siike Boots depend on GRID connectivity. No GRID network is required, as they connect directly to the user, but they will stop functioning if the user wears a GRID blocking bracelet.

It’s a Free Action to use the boots.

The Siike boots have 5 charges. Each charge can be used to double the wearer’s speed for one round (3 seconds).

If speeding through enemies, they can use a Reaction to Attack or Grapple the user but they do so with a 2d10 penalty to their roll.

Speed: The wearer’s Speed is doubled by using one charge.

Anti-Grapple: The wearer may add 2d10 to any Agility + Acrobatics check against a Grapple check (Athletics + Brute Force) by an enemy attempting to intercept them.
For example, a character uses the Siike Boots to speed through 2 enemies blocking their way in an alleyway. The two enemies the character speeds through get to use a Reaction to stop them (Grapple). Both roll Athletics + Brute Force vs. Agility + Acrobatics. If the wearer’s Agility is 3 and Acrobatics is 2, the wearer would roll 7d10 on their Agility + Acrobatics check to avoid being grappled.

3 charges are recovered in one phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or one hour in a charging point.

description: The Siike boots are imbued with Siike energy that bursts when triggered by the wearer’s GRID. They propel the wearer in the direction of their choosing.
name: Siike Boots (Premium)
usage:
The Siike Boots depend on GRID connectivity. No GRID network is required, as they connect directly to the user, but they will stop functioning if the user wears a GRID blocking bracelet.

It’s a Free Action to use the boots.

The Siike boots have 5 charges. Each charge can be used to double the wearer’s speed for one round (3 seconds).

If speeding through enemies, they can use a Reaction to Attack or Grapple the user but they do so with a 2d10 penalty to their roll.

The Premium model allows the user to stack charges.

Speed: The wearer’s Speed is doubled by using one charge, then each additional “stacked” charge adds 25% of half the doubled speed on top. For example, if the wearer’s Speed is 12, the wearer can use a Free Action to spend 3 charges, making the character’s Speed 33 for one turn (24 after doubling, 6 for the second charge, and 3 more for the third).

Anti-Grapple: The wearer may add 2d10 per charge used to an Agility + Acrobatics check against a Grapple check (Athletics + Brute Force) by an enemy attempting to intercept them.
For example, a character uses 3 charges to speed through 2 enemies blocking their way in an alleyway. The two enemies the character speeds through get to use a Reaction to stop him (Grapple). Both roll Athletics + Brute Force vs. Agility + Acrobatics. If the wearer’s Agility is 3 and Acrobatics is 2, the wearer would roll 11d10 on their Agility + Acrobatics check to avoid being grappled.

5 charges are recovered in one phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or one hour in a charging point.

description: This nano-drone is minute: half a Human thumb. It hums almost imperceptibly when in mint state.
name: Sphly (regular)
notes:
The Sphly is so small, it would take 5 Successes on one Attack Roll (Range) to take it down.

Alternatively, if in melee range, one can catch it with 3 Successes on an Attack Roll (Melee), in which case it can be turned off rather than broken (each failed attempt adds 1d10 penalty on any subsequent attempt).

The Sphly can be reprogrammed with a Deduction + Software OR GRID thus:

1 Success: sever connection with original user.
2 Successes: connect the Sphly to a new user.

The Sphly and its users must be registered in the Confederal Database for Advanced Technology Acquisitions (CODATA) under the Safe Use of Advanced Technology Act of 2063 .
usage:
A user can connect the Sphly to their GRID and programme it with a Deduction + Software OR GRID. Once connected, the user can give it a target or manually direct it.

If giving it a target, it must be within sight of the Sphly. It will lock visually to the target.

If directing it, make a Deduction or Mentality + GRID roll to see how well you “fly” it.

The Deduction or Mentality + GRID check needs to be repeated for every 1 km the Sphly moves away from the user. If the check fails, the connection with the Sphly is lost. The same check can be repeated to call it back. If it fails again, it floats away aimlessly until the battery drains.

The Sphly has 2 charges. Each gives 1 hour of flight. It regains one per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or both in one hour if connected to a charging port.

If controlling or “flying” the Sphly directly, the user can make a Mentality + GRID / Stealth / Sense check to avoid a character from spotting the Sphly with a Perception-based check.

description: This nano-drone is minute: half a Human thumb. It hums almost imperceptibly when in mint state.
name: Sphly (Premium)
notes:
The Sphly is so small, it would take 5 Successes on one Attack Roll (Range) to take it down.

Alternatively, if in melee range, one can catch it with 3 Successes on an Attack Roll (Melee), in which case it can be turned off rather than broken (each failed attempt adds 1d10 penalty on any subsequent attempt).

The Sphly can be reprogrammed with a Deduction + Software thus:

1 Success: sever connection with original user.
2 Successes: connect the Sphly to a new user.

The Sphly and its users must be registered in the Confederal Database for Advanced Technology Acquisitions (CODATA) under the Safe Use of Advanced Technology Act of 2063.
usage:
A user can connect the Sphly to their GRID and programme it with a Deduction + Software OR GRID. Once connected, the user can give it a target or manually direct it.

If giving it a target, it must be within sight of the Sphly. It will lock visually to the target.

If directing it, make a Deduction or Mentality + GRID roll to see how well you “fly” it.

The Deduction or Mentality + GRID check needs to be repeated for every 10 km the Sphly moves away from the user. If the check fails, the connection with the Sphly is lost. The same check can be repeated to call it back. If it fails again, it floats away aimlessly until the battery drains.

The Sphly has 4 charges. Each gives 1 hour of flight. It regains one per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or both in one hour if connected to a charging port.

If controlling or “flying” the Sphly directly, the user can make a Mentality + GRID / Stealth / Sense check to avoid a character from spotting the Sphly with a Perception-based check.

For a Premium model, the Sphly will automatically try to return to the last known location for the user if disconnected (if within battery range; otherwise, it will drop on its way there). Alternatively, it can be set to return to a designated location (if within range; otherwise, it will drop on its way there), or stay put.

description: This nano-drone is minute: half a Human thumb. It hums almost imperceptibly when in mint state.
name: Sphly (Premium Plus)
notes:
The Sphly is so small, it would take 5 Successes on one Attack Roll (Range) to take it down.

Alternatively, if in melee range, one can catch it with 3 Successes on an Attack Roll (Melee), in which case it can be turned off rather than broken (each failed attempt adds 1d10 penalty on any subsequent attempt).

The Sphly can be reprogrammed with a Deduction + Software thus:

1 Success: sever connection with original user.
2 Successes: connect the Sphly to a new user.

The Sphly and its users must be registered in the Confederal Database for Advanced Technology Acquisitions (CODATA) under the Safe Use of Advanced Technology Act of 2063.
usage:
A user can connect the Sphly to their GRID and programme it with a Deduction + Software OR GRID. Once connected, the user can give it a target or manually direct it.

If giving it a target, it must be within sight of the Sphly. It will lock visually to the target.

If directing it, make a Deduction or Mentality + GRID + 4d10 roll to see how well you “fly” it.

The Deduction or Mentality + GRID check needs to be repeated for every 25 km the Sphly moves away from the user. If the check fails, the connection with the Sphly is lost. The same check can be repeated to call it back. If it fails again, it floats away aimlessly until the battery drains.

The Sphly has 8 charges. Each gives 1 hour of flight. It regains one per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or both in one hour if connected to a charging port.

Alternatively, it can be set to return to a designated location (if within range; otherwise, it will find its way to the nearest safe spot for charging first, then resume its march), or stay put.

description: Two paired bracelets, connected to the user via GRID, designed to offer protection against Siike attacks.
name: Siike Bracelet Shield
notes: The Siike Bracelet Shield was conceived by Trillich Genk in 2085 for the Northern Federation Defence Force (NF/DF), and later adopted by the Confederal Armed Forces (CAF) in 2090.
usage:
As part of a Defence Reaction, a user can add 2d10 per spent charge to a Defence Roll against a Siike Attack. This can be done after the initial Defence roll (i.e., the player can roll their normal Defence and then roll 2d10 more if necessary).

The Siike Bracelet Shield has 5 charges, which it can regain over a Phase of exposure to the Beam, or by being plugged to a Siike source for 5 hours (1 per charge).

description: A gun designed to hold a significant amount of concentrated yet inert high-density Siike energy, triggered upon shooting (by injecting in the barrel a heavily refined dose of Sinum) to become explosive on impact.
name: Siike Gun
notes:
The Siike gun holds 20 Siike rounds. It recovers 5 per Phase of direct Beam exposure or 1 per hour when using a Siike charge point.

Confederal Army Grade weapon.

It must be connected to an authorised GRID user. Two successes on a Deduction + Software check necessary to unlock it. Three successes would allow the user to lock it to their account.

Illegal unless under contract and licensed by an official security, police or army force.
usage:
The attacker makes an Attack (Range) roll + 3d10.

Alternatively, if in melee, the attacker can make an Attack (Melee) roll + 1d10 to blast Siike against the target, dealing the normal 2d6 Siike damage on a hit.

Every 10m beyond the weapon's range incurs a -1d10 penalty (minimum 1d10). At 50% over the range, the weapon does not reach its target, regardless of the Attack (Range) roll result.

The Siike Gun has 20 uses. It recovers 5 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per one hour of plugging to a Siike charging point.

description: The invisibility suit uses metalenses and nanofins to scatter light while projecting the background image, effectively rendering the wearer invisible. The wearer still makes noise when moving.
name: Invisibility Suit
notes:
Any repair on the Invisibility Suit costs 5,000 CONF plus 1,000 CONF per HP lost.

Shadow Attackers: a character that has the Shadow Attacker feat as part of their path can use the feat without having to hide first if wearing an active and undamaged Invisibility Suit.
usage:
It takes 1-2 minutes to put on or take off the Invisibility Suit.

It takes 1 Reaction to activate and deactivate the suit, and the wearer will become invisible at the beginning of their next turn.

Stealthy: While the suit is active, the wearer has an additional 5d10 to any Stealth check and Defence Reaction. Additionally, their Stealth score is doubled.

Grade 5 Protection: In addition, even if the wearer has already used their Reaction during their turn, the suit still allows the wearer to roll 5d10 against the attacker’s Attack Roll.

Fragile: The Invisibility Suit has 5 HP. When hit, the Invisibility Suit takes damage first. For each point of damage it takes, its bonus to Stealth and Defence is lowered by 1. At 0HP, it is rendered useless. However, if inactive, only damage rolls above 10 HP affect the Invisibility Suit (the wearer takes damage as though they weren't wearing any armour).

Additionally, if the Invisibility Suit takes 15 HP or more of damage in one hit OR if it accumulates 10HP of damage after reaching 0HP, it is broken beyond repair.

description: The Underwater Suit allows the wearer to breathe underwater and withstand pressure levels beyond those a humanoid can endure.
name: Underwater Suit (Pro)
usage:
1 minute to put the suit on or take it off.

No damage taken up to 4 atmospheres (~30 metres).

5 Siike charges. Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

1 Siike charge allows the wearer to: 1) breathe underwater for one hour; 2) withstand an additional atmosphere without having to roll Resistance (External) + Resilience.

description: The Underwater Suit allows the wearer to breathe underwater and withstand pressure levels beyond those a humanoid can endure.
name: Underwater Suit (Ultimate)
usage:
1 minute to put the suit on or take it off.

No damage taken up to 5 atmospheres (~37 metres).

10 Siike charges. Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

1 Siike charge allows the wearer to: 1) breathe underwater for one hour; 2) withstand an additional atmosphere without having to roll Resistance (External) + Resilience.

description: A rare item, found mostly in the underworld in its roughest version. High-end versions do exist in the luxury fighting circuit.
name: Siike Gloves
notes: *The Survivalist and Anti-Hero Paths allow characters to take 2 non-weapon Attacks as an Action.
usage:
Non-Weapon Weapons: Attacks deal 1d6 and are considered non-weapon attacks.*

Charged Punches: Each glove has 3 charges. One charge can be used per hit to add 1d6 Siike damage. Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

Grapple Overcharge: Forgoing any remaining Reactions for the round, a character may use all their Actions (if more than one) to make a contested Athletics + Brute Force vs. Athletics + Agility/Acrobatics check to grapple another character and discharge all remaining Siike charges in both gloves on a successful grapple. 

The attacker receives half the damage dealt, which can be halved again with a successful Resistance (External) check.

In addition, the Siike Gloves give the wearer a plus 2d10 bonus to Defence Reactions, as they are incredibly resistant.

description: Assault drones are often used by law or and order agencies, but they are known to have been used as well by private firms, provided they don't use lethal force.
name: Assault Drone
notes:
5 Successes (can be cumulative) on a Deduction + Software roll can switch it off.

As part of the 5 Successes, it can be programmed to shoot and not shoot specific targets (GRID signature or other identifier needed).

It can be modified to have better weapons or other tools, or to have more hit points.
usage:
It is GRID controlled. Two Deduction + Software successes (they don’t need to be consecutive) will be necessary to find out how to sync to it if off.

When on auto mode, it attacks with 5d10. It has two attacks, dealing 1d6 Bullet damage each.

When on manual mode, the user can use an Action to make 2 GRID Attacks, adding 5d10 to each attack. The Attacks are still Range and deal 1d6 Bullet damage each.

The Assault Drone has a Speed of 10m.

It defends with 3d10.

In manual mode, the user can use a Reaction to make a GRID Defence roll to defend, with a 3d10 bonus. It still counts as a Defence Range reaction.

The Assault Drone has 10 HP. At 0, it falls to the ground and stops functioning. It can be repaired unless the blow that takes it out (including any fall damage) pushes it below twice its max HP (i.e. -10 HP).

The Assault Drone has 50 bullets.

description: A thin screen designed to hover just before the eye, the rest of the Bionocle wrapping itself around the wearer’s temple. There are many versions of the Bionocle, but it is mostly used to enhance the wearer’s perception beyond humanoid capabilities.
name: Bionocle (basic)
usage:
Connects to the user via GRID (one Reaction to activate).

The Bionacle (basic) allows the user to:

Isolate and identify a person in a crowd, facilitating GRID interaction (automatic GRID signature read upon visual contact).

Double distance at which the user can see.

Improved aim: 1d10 to Range Attacks based on sight if using one Reaction to aim.

Improved perception: 2d10 to Perception-based rolls depending on sight.

name: Bionocle (pro)

Isolate and identify a person in a crowd, facilitating GRID interaction (automatic GRID signature read upon visual contact, plus 1d10 to break into the target's GRID prior to GRID Attack).

Quadruple distance at which the user can see.

Improved aim: 2d10 to Range Attacks depending on sight if using one Reaction to aim.

Improved perception: 3d10 to Perception-based rolls depending on sight.

Seeing in darkness and heat map of anything within sight.

name: Bionocle (advanced)
notes: Advanced models can only be purchased upon registering into the Confederal Database for Advanced Technology Acquisitions (CODATA) as established in the Safe Use of Advanced Technology Act of 2063.

Multiply by 10 the distance at which the user can see.

Improved aim: 3d10 to Range Attacks depending on sight if using one Reaction to aim.

Improved perception: 4d10 to Perception-based rolls depending on sight.

Seeing in darkness and heat map of anything within sight.

Seeing through walls (up to 50-cm thickness).

Spotting active Invisibility Suits.

name: Bionocle (premium)
notes: Premium models can only be purchased upon registering into the Confederal Database for Advanced Technology Acquisitions (CODATA) as established in the Safe Use of Advanced Technology Act of 2063.

Improved perception: 5d10 to Perception-based rolls depending on sight.

Seeing in darkness and heat map of anything within sight.

Seeing through walls (up to 50-cm thickness).

Spotting active Invisibility Suits.

By expending 1 Action, the user can determine ONE of the following:

- A character's expertise in their trade (level and path, mechanically).

- A a character or creature's current HP.

- A character or creature's resistances (if any).

- A character or creature's vulnerabilities (if any).

description: A thin screen designed to hover just before the eye, the rest of the Bionocle wrapping itself around the wearer’s temple. There are many versions of the Bionocle, but it is mostly used to enhance the wearer’s perception beyond humanoid capabilities.
name: Bionocle (army-grade)
notes: Army-grade models are exclusively issued to military personnel with the appropriate permissions and under the relevant current legislation.

Connects to the user via GRID (one Reaction to activate).

The Bionacle (premium) allows the user to:

Isolate and identify a person in a crowd, facilitating GRID interaction (automatic GRID signature read upon visual contact, plus 4d10 to break into the target's GRID prior to GRID Attack).

Multiply by 10 the distance at which the user can see.

Improved aim: 6d10 to Range Attacks depending on sight if using one Reaction to aim.

Improved perception: 6d10 to Perception-based rolls depending on sight.

Seeing in darkness and heat map of anything within sight.

Seeing through walls (up to 1-metre thickness).

Spotting active Invisibility Suits.

By expending 1 Action, the user can determine ONE of the following:

- A character's expertise in their trade (level and path, mechanically).

- A a character or creature's current HP.

- A character or creature's resistances (if any).

- A character or creature's vulnerabilities (if any).

costInfo: This item is illegal and found exclusively in the black market. Thus, its price may vary massively depending on the circumstances.
description:
Its purpose is simple: the amplifier’s body changes the Siike energy’s composition at the last minute, allowing the weapon not to be affected by the more volatile result. The inverted funnel end ensures the shot is rapidly ejected. The combination of the clever design and robust materials makes the amplifier resistant to most damage. However, Siike amplifiers are notorious for breaking after some use, sometimes ruining the weapon they are attached to in the process.
It is designed to fit most guns and rifles thanks to its adjustable screwable end.
name: Siike Amplifier
notes: The Siike amplifier is thought to have been first introduced in the 2030s by the Siikers gang in Dryth’s Z-19 underworld. The metal and synthetic alloy used is robust but the item’s purpose makes it inherently unstable.
usage:
One Action to attach and detach the Siike Amplifier to a Siike weapon (it has no effect on non-Siike weapons).

The weapon’s Attack modifier increases by 2d10.

The weapon’s damage roll is doubled.

Directly after each use, the amplifier may break:

The total damage dealt in one attack becomes the target for the amplifier’s check: roll 1d100; if the roll is equal to or lower than the damage dealt, the amplifier breaks.

Additionally, if the damage dealt is higher than 20 HP, the weapon may break: roll 1d100; if the roll is equal to or lower than HALF the damage dealt, the weapon breaks.

Finally, if the damage is higher than 20 HP, the shooter must make an Athletics + Agility save:

2 Successes: the shooter remains in place, no negative effects.

1 Success: the shooter is pushed 5m backwards.

No Successes: the shooter is pushed 5m backwards and falls prone, losing one Reaction in the process (and must use half Speed to standing back up).

description: A handheld device with capacity to store and discharge Siike energy against the nearest target. Used almost exclusively for self-defence.
name: Siike Taser
notes:
It is only legal to use the Siike taser in circumstances in which self-defence is paramount for self-preservation.

The Siike taser requires a licence (pending favourable psychological assessment) must be registered to the user in the Confederal Database for Advanced Technology Acquisitions (CODATA) under the Safe Use of Advanced Technology Act of 2063.
usage:
One Action to shoot.

Automatic hit if a target is in the 2-metre range (always targets the nearest character or creature).

The target makes an Agility + Acrobatics. Two Successes halve the damage

The Siike Taser has 5 charges. Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

areaEffect.successfulDefenceResult: Deduct 1d4 from the damage roll per Success on the Agility + Acrobatics Reaction roll.

costInfo: Cost per grenade.
description: An old-school grenade: the device explodes, sending shrapnel flying in every direction. Dirty but powerful.
name: Grenade
notes:
Grenades are banned in the Second Confederation, including for military use. In the 2100s, grenades are often only seen in the underworld and the most violent parts of the Buffer.

Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage: Athletics + Aim to throw it in the desired location.

costInfo: Cost per standard 10m of rope. Smart Siike Ropes can be connected to each other.
description: The Smart Siike Rope can be thrown against a target and it will do its best to accomplish the user’s stated intent: tie itself around an object, restrain a person, etc.
name: Smart Siike Rope (standard)
notes:
Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage:
Connect to the rope via GRID to obtain the +5 bonus

Make an Athletics + Aim + 3 (if the user is connected to the rope via GRID) roll. On a success, the rope ties itself around the target selected by the user. On a failure, the rope ties itself around an object located according to a failure under the Throw mechanics.

Additionally, if the rope is thrown against a living creature, the target can make a Defence Range roll to avoid it: if the Defence roll is equal or higher than the Attack roll, the rope ties around itself next to the creature.

1 Success on a Brute Force check allow the restrained target to break out of it: if successful, the Siike Rope breaks beyond repair.

costInfo: Cost per standard 10m of rope. Smart Siike Ropes can be connected to each other.
description:
The Smart Siike Rope can be thrown against a target and it will do its best to accomplish the user’s stated intent: tie itself around an object, restrain a person, etc.

The premium version comes with a detachable handle that can be used to lock and unlock the rope, call the rope back or trigger up to 3 Siike discharges on the target.
name: Smart Siike Rope (premium)
notes:
Using discharges from a Siike Rope on a sentient being is illegal in the Second Confederation and punishable under the Torture Abolition Act of 1903.

Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage:
Connect to the rope via GRID to obtain the +5 bonus

Make an Athletics + Aim + 5 (if the user is connected to the rope via GRID) roll. On a success, the rope ties itself around the target selected by the user. On a failure, the rope ties itself around an object 1d8 (1 is north, 8 is north-west) and 1d10 metres away from the target.

Additionally, if the rope is thrown against a living creature, the target can make a Defence Range roll to avoid it: if the Defence roll is equal or higher than the Attack roll, the rope ties around itself next to the creature.

Torturous. The premium version comes with 3 Siike charges. For each charge, the user can deal 1d6 physical damage on a tied target (the target must have been restrained by the rope first). Recover 3 charges per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

Unbreakable. The premium version allows the user to lock the rope, essentially making the knot impossible to break unless the user unlocks it with the handle. If a restrained target tries to physically break out of the rope, a Siike discharge is triggered for every attempt. Mechanically, every Brute Force check triggers a 1d6 Siike damage roll while the Siike Rope has charges. Only when the Siike Rope is out of charges may 1 Success on a  Brute Force check allow the restrained target to break out of it: if successful, the Siike Rope breaks beyond repair.

The premium plus version comes with a detachable handle that can be used to lock and unlock the rope, call the rope back or trigger up to 5 Siike discharges on the target.
name: Smart Siike Rope (premium plus)
notes:
Using discharges from a Siike Rope on a sentient being is illegal in the Second Confederation and punishable under the Torture Abolition Act of 1903.

description: A metallic receptacle full of highly corrosive acid inside an aerodynamic design to help the throw. Upon activation, a series of sprinklers open, spraying acid around.
name: Acid Bomb
notes:
Acid bombs are banned in the Second Confederation under the Weapon Safety and Fair Use Act of 1903.

In the 2100s, acid bombs are only used by gangs in the Buffer and the underworld.

Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage:
1 Action to activate and throw (Atheltics + Aim + 2d10).

Upon activation, at the end of the thrower's turn, the acid bomb goes off, spraying acid on a 10-metre radius.

Anyone in range can make an Agility + Acrobatics check to halve the acid damage. Those who fail the check take an additional 1d4 acid damage for the next 3 turns.

description: Viscous and white, it seems to slosh around the flask of its own accord. The flask contains 1 litre (10 doses) of the Vitocatalyst.
name: Vitocatalyst
notes: The Vitocatalyst was first developed by Dr Orega Plynth in her secret laboratory. She made a fortune by selling it in exclusivity to Shanside University, which boosted the reputation of the latter, giving it an advantage in Biology Studies for the next three decades. During experimentation, Dr Plynth shared the substance with other doctors, such as Dr Cyan Yde in the Dryth underworld.
usage: Make a Science + Deduction cumulative roll and expend 1 dose per roll to investigate the Vitocatalyst at the GM's discretion.

description: The nanofibres that comprise this armour are quantum-entangled to fibres stored in immense facilities. When the quantum armour is hit, the kinetic energy is instantaneously transferred to the power plant. The energy is transferred into tanks and engines—the “damage storage facilities” are said to be self-powered. In the Northern Federation, where power is always scarce, this is seen as the ultimate act of efficiency: protecting soldiers anywhere absorbs damage into the power plants. The facilities are also connected to the main power grid.
name: Quantum Armour
notes:
This is the latest experimental tech at the hands only of the most advanced Northern Federation soldiers.

Nobody outside NF scientists would know how to fix it. Even if they figured it out, they would need NF pass codes to resume the quantum entanglement with NF damage stations.
usage:
It takes at least 5 minutes to put it on and take it off.

The armour has a 10 HP threshold, beyond which, the damage gets to the wearer. Effectively, it shaves off 10 HP from any hit.

The armour has 20 HP. Only hits above 20 HP actually hit the armour. Any damage above 20 HP damages the armour. Once it goes below 10 HP, the HP threshold is reduced accordingly. For example, if the armour has been heavily damaged and only has 4 HP left, it can only shave off 4 HP from hits.

It acts as Grade 4 armour as far as Defence Rolls are concerned.

The wearer’s Agility and Athletic has 1d10 penalty per Agility and Athletic score under 4 (for example, if the wearer’s Agility is 3, it has 1d10 penalty to any Agility checks and saves; if it is 2, it has a 2d10 penalty).

costInfo: This item is illegal and found exclusively in the black market. Thus, its price may vary massively depending on the circumstances.
description: The modified multi-damage rifle is found in several forms in the black market, mostly in the underworld and the Buffer. It has been crafted to allow users to attack all manner of threats and deal more damage at the expense of accuracy and range.
name: Modified Multi-Damage Rifle
notes:
Modified multi-damage rifles are banned in the Second Confederation under the Weapon Safety and Fair Use Act of 1903.

In the 2100s, they are only used by gangs in the Buffer and the underworld.
usage:
The modified multi-damage rifle has a range 30m when set to Siike damage.

The modified multi-damage rifle has 5 Siike shots, 3 fire shots, 3 acid shots and 3 ice shots. They all deal 1d10 damage.

For acid, fire and ice, a hit deals another 1d4 from the hit target’s next turn for 5 rounds. Each hit has its own 5 round countdown during which it continues to deal 1d4 of that type of damage at the beginning of the hit target’s turn. Therefore, a target could start a turn and receive 2d4 acid damage plus 1d4 fire, if it has been hit twice by an acid shot and once by a fire shot and they are still within the 5 rounds of each.

The victim can make a free Resistance (External) roll per battle to halve additional damage (the 1d4 rounds) on a per damage type basis. For instance, the first time a target is hit with acid, they can make a Resistance (External) check to halve any 1d4 additional damage they may take that battle without having to roll if they're hit with acid again. If they were hit with ice next, they would roll once more to try and halve any additional ice damage. Normal hits will continue to deal damage of any type as normal.

It takes 2 Reactions to switch damage type on the modified multi-damage rifle.

description: The Siike rifle is used by armed forces in high-stakes operations. It offers extensive range and accuracy, and devastating damage.
name: Siike Rifle
notes:
The Siike rifle holds 10 Siike rounds. It recovers 3 per Phase of direct Beam exposure or 1 per hour when using a Siike charge point.

Confederal Army Grade weapon.

It must be connected to an authorised GRID user. Two successes on a Deduction + Software check necessary to unlock it. Three successes would allow the user to lock it to their account.

Illegal unless under contract and licensed by an official security, police or army force.
usage:
For range attacks: 3d10 bonus to attack.

Alternatively, if in melee, the attacker can make an Attack (Melee) roll + 1d10 to blast Siike against the target, dealing the normal 3d6 Siike damage on a hit.

Every 10m beyond the weapon's range incurs a -1d10 penalty (minimum 1d10). At 50% over the range, the weapon does not reach its target, regardless of the Attack (Range) roll result.

Heavy: The user must have at least a 3 in Athletics or Brute Force to carry this weapon or incur a penalty of 1d10 for each point below 3 whenever using it.

The Siike Rifle has 20 uses. Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

costInfo: This is a highly illegal device found in the underworld. The danger it poses has made it a bit of a relic even there. Price may vary massively.
description:
Deduction + Mechanics / Software / GRID Check to recognise the object (if unknown).
This is a chunky bit of old-school tech. One suspects its size is due to the vast amount of energy needed to blast into a GRID chip.
The GRID Splicer is supposed to have two main uses: to blast into someone else’s GRID and read or modify it, and to program inserted chips with information from one’s own.
name: GRID Splicer
usage:
GRID Hijacker:

Deduction + Software + 2d10 to detect a GRID chip. The target may make a Perception + GRID check to notice the connection attempt.

Deduction + GRID + 2d10 on a detected GRID chip. If the target perceives the Attack, they can defend with a Mentality + GRID + GRID Defence Patch Mod (if present).

If the GRID Attack is successful, the target takes 3d6 Mental damage. If it’s not, the attacker makes a GRID Defence roll or takes 3d6 Mental damage.

If the Attack is successful, the attacker can read the target’s chip for 1 minute before the GRID auto-defence lockdown mechanism kicks in (modified chips may require another Mentality + GRID + GRID Defence Patch Mod if present or be open for another minute).

Alternatively, the Attacker may shorten the reading time to try to insert information in the target’s GRID: this can be inserting or modifying something the target knows or remembers. This use is highly experimental and deals an additional 3d6 damage on the target, who must also get a Success on a Mentality + GRID roll or take 1 Serious Injury.

GRID Chip Programmer:

Insert a GRID chip into the GRID Splicer.

Make a Deduction + Software check to tune into the GRID chip through your GRID, enter edit mode and access the chip’s unique system encryption. (2 Successes necessary unless the character has a Software OR GRID score of 4 or 5, in which case, 1 Success suffices.)

If successful in the previous step, make a Deduction + GRID + GRID Modifying/Editing Patch (if any):

1 Success: You manage to insert a piece of information into the chip.

2 Successes: You can modify one piece of existing information in the chip.

3 Successes: You can install a patch into the chip OR insert two bits of information OR modify two pieces of information in the chip (alternatively, you can insert one and modify another).

areaEffect.successfulDefenceResult: Subtracts 1d6 off the damage per Success.

description: The Siike Club is a weapon often used in gang fights in the underworld and the Buffer. Perfect for one-on-one battles, it gives the user the possibility of using Siike energy as part of their attacks. This version also allows for huge Siike blasts that take everything and everyone in front of the attacker.
name: Siike Club
notes:
A new Siike Club has a 1% breakage probability. A fully-repaired one goes back to 11%.
*This is an illegal item, designed by the Siikers’ master craftspeople. The price could vary significantly as the item can only be acquired in the black market.
** A broken Siike club can still be used as a melee weapon but the user gets no plus 2d10 to hit and the damage is only 1d6.
usage:
A character needs a Brute Force attribute of at least 2 to wield the Siike Club as a weapon.

In addition to allowing the user to perform Melee Attack swith a 2d10 bonus, dealing 2d6 damage, the Siike Club has 5 charges (recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point), which can be used to blast a Siike Cone (see below).

Siike Cone: The user can use their Action and Reactions (this doesn’t apply to class-based Reactions) to hold the Siike Club in front of an enemy or group of enemies and discharge a wave of accumulated Siike, dealing 5d6 damage to anyone within a 5-metre cone originating from the club (a target may use a Reaction to make an Agility + Acrobatics check, subtracting 1d6 for each rolled Success). Roll for breakage (see below).

Breakage Probability: The Siike club may break when using it to launch a Siike Cone. I starts with a 5% breakage probability. Right after using a Siike Cone, roll 1d100. If the roll is below the item’s breakage probability, it breaks and must be repaired. If it doesn’t break, add 10% to its breakage probability. If adding to the breakage probability reaches or exceeds 100%, the Siike Club breaks and must be repaired before it can be used again (see below).

Repairable: The Siike Club can be repaired for an approximate cost of 200 CONF per 10% of breakage probability reduction. A full repair once broken costs around 2,500 CONF and sets the breakage probability to 10%. (See price information).

description: The Siike Whip is often used in gang fights in the underworld and the Buffer. By harnessing the power of Siike energy and its elastic design, it allows a melee fighter to attack at range, discharging Siike energy against enemies who cannot retort.
name: Siike Whip
notes: The Siike Whip normally has 5 charges, after which it must be recharged (1 hour at a Siike source to fully replenish, or 1 charge per phase if exposed to the Beam).
usage:
The Siike Whip allows the user to perform Melee Attacks up to 15 metres of range with a 3d10 Attakc bonus; the Siike Whip will automatically fail to hit if the target is beyond 15m.

In addition, the target can use a Reaction to make an Athletics/Agility + Brute Force/Acrobatics check or be held by the Siike Whip. If the target is still held at the beginning of their next turn, they take an additional 1d6 Siike damage.

While holding a target with the Siike Whip, the user can attempt to pull the target 5m towards the user with a contested Brute Force + Athletics check.

The Siike Whip has 10 uses. Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

description: The Siike hammer is charged with Siike energy, which gives it more power. It can also be used to lock it to a Siike source as an anchor, or to break through materials often resistant to brute force hammering.
name: Siike Hammer
usage:
If used to attack, add 2d10 if Melee (no bonus to hit if throwing it), and it deals 1d6 bluedgeoning damage on a Brute Force + Melee success.

The hammer has 5 Siike charges, which can be expended to deal an additional 1d6 Siike damage (the user decides how many charges they expend on the attack on a hit). Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

By expending a charge, the Siike hammer can be anchored to a Siike source. This is sometimes used to tie a rope or restrain something or somebody.

If trying to break through a strengthened material (such a silicone-steel hybrid), one charge can be expended to break the molecular bindings and deal 1d6 damage like it would on normal materials.

description: Siikemeters detect Siike in the vicinity and offer an analysis of its proximity, intensity and composition. The closer the device is to a Siike source, the more accurate its readings.
name: Siikemeter
notes:
A Siike bath defaults to a composition of 13% Sinum, 50% Ikium and 37% Ette.

Higher levels of Sinum often indicate volatility.

Siike crystallises at 33.3% levels of each element, which is almost impossible to achieve even in a lab due to the high volatility of such high Sinum levels.

Siike guns are designed to increase Sinum levels to above 50% just before the energy leaves the barrel, causing it to detonate on impact.
usage: Point the bottom or top of the device toward the area you want to analyse.

costInfo: Only available in the underworld, or from those connected to Dr Cyan Yde.
description:
The substance is semi-viscous but easy enough to swallow. It appears to trigger and expedite the body's natural response to damage, effectively inducing the user to self-heal.

A flask contains one use.
name: Yde’s Aid
notes: This an illegal, experimental concoction by Dr Cyan Yde that hasn’t been tested other than on Cyber Act members. Unknown side effects may result from consuming it.
usage:
1 Action to consume, as the liquid has a tendency to stick to one’s throat.

The drinker regains 2d4 HP as internal wounds and ruptured tissues are compelled to heal.

description:
The nanobot medical kit allows the user to perform quick healing operations on a subject on the field. Originally conceived to help quick healing operations in theatres of conflict, nanobot medical kits are now available for police forces and other high-risk jobs.

The nanobots are applied to a wound via direct contact with the skin to ensure they suture the right area.
name: Nanobot Medical Kit
notes: A standard kit has 5 uses. It can be recharged at a specialist nanobot company for 2,500 CONF.
usage:
By following the instructions a character not trained in Medicine (no points in the Medicine skill), can spend their entire turn Speed, Action and any Reactions to heal themselves or another character for 1d6 Physical HP if they succeed (1 success) on a Maual/Deduction + Medicine check.

Trained professionals (must have at least 1 point in the Medicine skill) only need to use their Action and always heal themselves or another character 1d6 Physical HP (guaranteed).

If they have the Doctor/Scientist path, as part of this healing Action, they can roll a Manual + Medicine check. They can add 1d6 Physical HP healing for every Success rolled, up to a maximum of their Medicine skill score.*

*For instance, if a character has a 2 in Manual and a 3 in Medicine, they can make a Manual + Medicine check (5d10). If they roll 2 Successes, they would heal for 3d6 (1 guaranteed + 2 Successes. If they rolled 4 Successes, they would heal 4d6 (1 guaranteed + 3 out of the 4 Successes, because 3 is their maximum as ruled by the Medicine skill).

The Naobot MedKit has 5 uses To recover uses, the character must purchase a recharge (500 CONF per use)

description: Siike Stars are used by police forces (notably the NCP) as part of raids into criminal headquarters. They allow officers to burst into tight spaces and shoot against known targets while performing other actions.
name: Siike Star
notes:
Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage:
It takes 1 Action to throw 2 Siike Stars. You can forfeit any Reactions to throw a third one.

Each Siike Star has 10 charges and uses one of them for each shot. It then recharges 1 per hour if exposed to the Beam or 5 per hour if on a charger.

The Siike Star uses one charge to shoot against a given target or location. The Attack is 5d10. The atrget can make a Defence (Range) to avoid being hit.

The Siike Stars are just a tad bigger than an average Human’s hand. They can be thrown into a space and they’ll attach themselves to a surface. They are connected to the user’s GRID. The user can set a target for it, using a Reaction to change targets.

The Siike Stars shoot at the beginning of the user's next turn following activation and target setting.

costInfo: 50 CONF afford 5 salve uses.
description: Some types of damage continue to deal damage on their target. These are potent salves that stop such a type of damage from recurring.
name: Specific Damage Salve
notes: Known types are fire, acid and ice burns.
usage:
1 Action per application to stop 1d4 recurring damage.
The salves have a limited number of uses.

costInfo: Only available in the underworld and the roughest parts of the Buffer.
description: A nasty piece of work, the barbed chain is used in gang fights, allowing the user to attack at a certain range and inflict damage without going one-on-one.
name: Barbed Chain
usage:
The user can use 1 Action to perform an Attack (Melee) with a 3d10 bonus, dealing 1d6 Blade damage on a hit.

On a hit, the target may use another Reaction (in addition to the defence to avoid being hit) to make an Agility + Acrobatics save. On a failure (or if out of Reactions or choosing not to use one), they take an additional 1d6 damage as the Barbed Chain tears at their skin when pulled by the attacker.

description: Siike-resistant overalls are common around Siike extraction sites and Siike refinement plants. They can be worn over clothes and armour alike due to their elasticity and the intelligent fibres with which they are confectioned.
name: Siike Resistant Overalls
usage:
At least one minute needed to put them on.

The Overalls have 10 HP.

While wearing them and while the overall have HP left, any Siike damage is halved.

A shooter aiming at a target wearing the Siike-resistant overalls may try to bypass them by incurring a 4d10 penalty to their Attack (aiming for the head or any other exposed areas). Additionally, certain feats may allow the shooter to bypass the overalls.

costInfo: This item is illegal and found exclusively in the black market. Thus, its price may vary massively depending on the circumstances.
description: A modified rifle crafted by gangs in the underworld and the Buffer to inflict one of the worst kinds of lasting damage: acid.
name: Modified Acid Rifle
notes: This item is illegal and found exclusively in the black market.
usage:
The user can make an Attack (Range) with a 3d10 bonus, dealing 2d6 Acid damage on a hit.

Continuous Damage: If hit, the target takes an additional 1d4 acid damage per round starting at the beginning of its next turn, for a maximum of 5 rounds. Any additional hit adds 1d4 for another 5 rounds.** The additional damage can be cured with an anti-acid salve.

The rifle has 10 rounds before it needs to be refilled (it takes a full Action and turn to do so).

Every 10 metres beyond the Modified Acid Rifle's range incurs a 1d10 penalty on the Attack (Range) roll.

**Each hit adds 1d4 for 5 rounds. Each hit has its own round counter for additional damage. Thus, if a character is hit on round 1, they take 2d6 on the hit, and 1d4 at the beginning of their next turn. On round 2, they are not hit by the acid, but when their turn comes, they take 1d4 damage. On round 3, they are hit by acid again, so they take 2d6. Additionally, when their turn comes, they take 2d4 acid damage (from both hits). On round 6, if they haven't been hit by acid again, they would take 1d4 acid from the round 3 hit but the effect of the round 1 hit is gone.

costInfo:
Not for sale, Dr Yde’s experiment.

Price is the latest known to have been sold for in Z-19's underworld for one dose.
description: This incredibly powerful and advanced chemical concoction modifies certain cerebral synapses and wires them in a way that makes the consumer susceptible to understanding most animal communication.
name: FaunCall
notes: This is Dr Yde’s experimental drug and hasn’t been tested properly.
usage:
It takes 1 Reaction to drink (1 Action to administer to someone else).
For 1 hour, the consumer can understand most animal communication and can add 2d10 to Persuasion + Survival checks to communicate with most animals.

costInfo:
Not for sale, Dr Yde’s experiment.

Price is the latest known to have been sold for in Z-19's underworld for one dose.
description: A strange green liquid swirls inside a sleek, tiny tube. It can be drunk in one sip. The consumer is temporarily imbued with unnatural speed.
name: Speedo
notes: Similar official products are known to have been used by Neblir’s Confederal Police (NCP) and Neblir’s Special Forces (NSF), but Speedo is an underworld alternative and thus illegal to consume or trade.
usage:
Ingest the liquid (1 Reaction, or 1 Action to administer it to somebody else).

The consumer’s speed is increased by 50%, and they have an additional Action and Reaction for 1 minute (10 turns). In addition, Defence Reactions have a 1d10 bonus.

Once the effect is over, the consumer feels lethargic: their speed is reduced by 50% and they lose all Reactions for 1 minute (10 turns).

costInfo: Illegal item found in the black market.
description: The intended use for the Siike Funnel is to charge devices with Siike energy from various sources. The funnel part has a universal adapter so it can attach itself to a few different devices such as large Siike batteries.
name: Siike Funnel
notes: In the underworld, a notorious use is to drain weapons into explosive devices. These explosive devices are notoriously unstable too (at the GM's discretion).
usage:
The Siike funnel takes Siike energy from any devices, neutralising it for storage and future use.

The funnel can neutralise up to 10 charges in one hour. For any additional charge to be funneled within the hour, make a 1d100. On a 10 or lower, the funnel breaks. Every new check increases the probability by 5%.

costInfo: Not for sale. Experimental technology. Price will vary massively for any found in the black market.
description: It looks like a watch, a bracelet with a screen. This device is experimental science at its best: when activated, it reads and processes the motions of those around it at lightning speed. Its AI then offers an uncannily accurate prediction of everybody’s movements.
name: ChronoBlink
notes: Extremely rare device. It is believed NF Fenne inventors are behind the tech, but it has not been approved for official use by any armed force in the Second Confederation.
usage:
3 charges. One phase of charging from a Siike port gives one charge back.

The effect lasts until the end of the user’s next Turn.

One Reaction (can be called at any point during a round) to use a charge and activate it (the effect lasts until the end of the user’s next turn).

It offers a glimpse into the immediate future, the next couple of seconds. The character can then use their Movement, Action (it can be held too) or any remaining Reaction at any point in the Initiative order. For instance, if an enemy approaches the character wearing the activated ChronoBlink and makes an Attack, the wearer can negate that by using their Movement, saying they moved elsewhere beforehand; if an enemy Attacks an ally, the wearer can jump Initiative and shoot the enemy (using their Action for that round) or push away their ally (using a Reaction) before the enemy attacks.

Additionally, while the ChronoBlink is active, the user adds 4d10 to both Attack and Defence rolls as they anticipate enemies’ movements, and while active, the wearer does not incur Attacks of Opportunity.

Finally, the wearer can use a charge to decide where in the Initiative order they want to be placed after rolling Initiative. This use doesn’t cost a Reaction as it is part of the Initiative roll.

description: A partially synthetic, partially organic chip built and programmed for each individual shortly after birth. Hardly the size of an ancient micro SIM card. Semi-translucent filaments extend from its contours and attach to the user’s blood vessels and soft tissue. Thus, a GRID chip is precisely tailored not only at race or gender level but bespoke to each individual.
name: GRID Chip
notes:
The vast majority of people will not change their GRID chip. They don’t even think about it. GRID chips are highly regulated. They are a person’s passport and financial record. They give one unlimited access – but it’s a two-way street: you can access everything and everything can access you.

Illegal GRID chips exist, especially in the Buffer and in the Outer Confederation. Criminals may also resort to them to escape law enforcement. The penalties for using an illegal GRID chip are only lower than those given to defendants guilty of manufacturing and selling them. Dealing GRID chips is akin to dealing drugs, smuggling weapons or being involved in rape or murder.
usage:
Instructions for someone attempting to create or adapt a GRID chip:

Procure a sample blank chip from a (preferably approved) manufacturer: Roll Investigation + Research (1 attempt per day) => 2 cumulative successes

Ensure you specify race, sex and age (if setting it up yourself, roll – 1 attempt per day – Deduction + Software=> 2 cumulative successes)

Ensure you include blood and DNA samples for maximum compatibility (if doing this yourself, roll – 1 attempt per day – Deduction + Science => 2 cumulative successes)

Only a Doctor/Scientist path character should attempt this (consequences of a non-trained character doing so may have consequences at the GM's discretion). To implant the GRID chip on the subject’s wrist => roll Manual + Medicine:

No Successes: The chip doesn’t connect.
1 Success: The chip connects (no need to tell the Player, but an infection ensues, and it gets worse every day: Resistance (Internal) Success OR 1d6 damage
2 Successes: The chip connects (but glitches at the GM’s discretion)
3 Successes: A proper connection, professional.

description: Slightly smaller than a traffic cone, this item is some of the latest tech utilised by police and other law enforcement officers in the Second Confederation. By placing it near officers, any enemies or criminals shooting Siike guns have their shots deflected.
name: Siike Deflector
usage:
One Action to place and activate.
Once active, it imposes a 3d10 penalty on Attacks involving Siike weapons against anyone within a 10m radius from the Siike Deflector.

costInfo: The necrocatalyst is exceedingly regulated and only available for purchase by official medical institutions and universities.
description:
This necrocatalyst agent is normally used in autopsies and other post-mortem procedures to temporarily imbue inert bodies with an pseudo-life energy of sorts. It can drive other chemicals and nano-bots through dead blood vessels as well as shock dead brains into a final burst of activity.
It could technically be used to imbue life-sucking agents with more potency.
name: Necrocatalyst
notes: A necrocatalyst bottle (0.5 litres) contains 10 doses.
usage:
Every time the researcher tries to analyse the necrocatalyst (Deduction + Research roll, as explained below), they must spend one dose and one day (spending an additional dose gives an extra die to the roll):

5 cumulative Deduction + Research/Science successes allow the researcher to use the necrocatalyst to imbue a “death shot” with more force: 1 dose gives 1d6 (10% rounded up) extra damage.

10 cumulative Deduction + Research/Science successes allow the researcher to create a dose of “un-life”: it brings a recently dead person to “un-life”, allowing them to answer questions for a minute (5 questions) (spending 5 necrocatalyst doses, 1,000 CONF in additional chemicals and 2 days). The researcher can now also imbue a “death shot” with 2d6 additional damage per dose used.

15 cumulative Deduction + Research/Science successes allow the researcher to create necrocatalyst: they can make 5 doses of necrocatalyst in 2 days by spending 1,000 CONF in materials and chemicals.

20 cumulative Deduction + Research/Science successes allow the researcher to replicate the necrocatalyst at will (spending 150 CONF and one day per dose). The researcher can now also create a shot of “un-life'' at will (spending 2 necrocatalyst doses, 750 CONF and 5 days).

20+ Deduction + Research/Science: you realise you have a level of command of this substance akin only to those who have studied it for months. How you manage it to play with life and death is up to your creativity.

description: A sleek, plain-looking bracelet with a screen that’s only visible to the wearer if attuned to it via GRID.
name: GRID Blocking Bracelet (Regular)
notes: Highly unusual in the Second Confederation in 2096. Often only seen in intelligence agents or the most sophisticated criminals. They will gradually become more available, and cheaper, although still mostly illegal from the 2120s.
usage:
One Action to use in Initiative.

For the regular model, the GRID is blocked. The wearer cannot use the GRID. They can also not be contacted or traced. A feeling of nausea is often associated with cheaper models.

description: A sleek, plain-looking bracelet with a screen that’s only visible to the wearer if attuned to it via GRID.
name: GRID Blocking Bracelet (Advanced)
notes: Highly unusual in the Second Confederation in 2096. Often only seen in intelligence agents or the most sophisticated criminals. They will gradually become more available, and cheaper, although still mostly illegal from the 2120s.
usage:
One Action to use in Initiative. Connect to the bracelet via GRID.

The user's GRID is blocked. They can also not be contacted or traced.

The advanced model offers a more pleasant transition to GRID-less mode. It can also only partially block the GRID, allowing the wearer to access certain databases or GRIDs blocked by the same protocol, or previously set private networks to allow communication among specific users.

description: The software patch is installed in the user’s GRID. It allows the user to look at a food item and check whether the item is safe to eat or not.
name: GRID Edibility Patch
notes: The software can be downloaded via GRID or applied via wrist in a shop.
usage: Once the software has been installed, the user can look at a food item and learn the probability that it may be edible or, if not, the potential dangers to the intaker’s health. If in Initiative (e.g. while in combat), the user must use one Action to ascertain the edibility of a food item.

description: The software patch is installed in the user’s GRID. It allows the user to see in the dark in hues of green.
name: GRID Night Vision Patch
notes: The software can be downloaded via GRID or applied via wrist in a shop.
usage: Once the software has been installed, the user can see in the dark for up to 50 metres.

description: Army-grade device to incapacitate anyone susceptible to sound.
name: Sonic Disruptor
usage:
Activate costs one Action. Set the device, press the button. There’s a timer too. Once it goes off, any susceptible being in a 25m-radius loses their Movement and Action for 1 minute. After 6 seconds (one round), an affected character can make Resistance (External) + Resilience roll. On a success, they regain Movement, but their Action is still spent covering their ears (and Defence Reactions have a 4d10 penalty, minimum 1d10 roll). If uncovering their ears to perform an Action, the process is repeated (they become incapacitated after they perform the Action).

Two charges. Once used up, it must charge for 10 hours (1 Phase).

The device is known to reach a 50m-radius if turned to max, but this may cause it to break. If used at max capacity, roll 1d100: on a 25 or lower, the device breaks.

description: A professional knife used by professional explorers, reinforced with Siike energy, including a small battery that empowers the blade at the user's request.
name: Siike Explorer’s Knife
usage:
Make a Melee Attack + 2d10, dealing 1d6 Blade damage.

The knife has 3 Siike charges, which can be used when an attack hits (the attacker chooses how many). The knife needs 1 Phase to recover (if directly exposed to the Beam, one day if not) or one hour in a Siike charging point. Each charge deals an extra 1d6 Siike damage.

description: The device gives GRID signal for a 5-km radius from the nearest GRID reach point.
