name: GRID Signal Booster
usage: Activate the GRID booster. It has 10 charges and uses 1 per hour of signal boosting. If exposed to the Beam, it recovers one charge per phase. It can charge continuously via GRID but the signal boosting is reduced to 5 km when thus operated. GRID Signal boosters can be chained to one another to increase reach.

costInfo: A CalmX dose costs 50 CONF.
description:
The injector comes with 10 doses and is programmed not to allow the same user to take more than 1 per day. It can be recharged at a pharmacy with a medical prescription.
CalmX is an extreme sedative. It is also highly addictive.
name: CalmX Injector
usage:
It takes one Action to inject oneself. CalmX calms the consumer and restores 1d6 Mental HP.

Addictive: A user who takes a dose per day for 5 consecutive days, more than one dose on the same day, or 6 doses within the same week risks addiction.

Roll a Mentality + Resistance (Internal) save every time you use CalmX once you meet the conditions above. On a failure, CalmX calms you but doesn't restore Mental HP. Once addicted, you must take a shot every day or suffer 1d3 Mental damage every day. Addiction can be treated by a professional at a suitable clinic.

description: A custom-made foldable knife, especifically desgined for a two-thumbed Fenne hand. The sleekness of its design makes it more lethal than a common knife. In addition, it is powered by an internal Siike battery, allowing the user to blast Siike as part of an attack.
name: Siike Fenne Foldable Knife
notes: This is a rare Fenne item, usually commissioned on request.
usage:
Make a Melee Attack + 3d10, dealing 2d6 Blade damage.

The knife has 3 Siike charges, which can be used when an attack hits (the attacker chooses how many). The knife needs 1 Phase to recover (if directly exposed to the Beam, one day if not) or one hour in a Siike charging point. Each charge deals an extra 1d6 Siike damage.

description: A common knife for an explorer of the wilder parts of Neblir.
name: Common Explorer’s Knife
usage: Make a Melee Attack + 2d10, dealing 1d6 Blade damage.

description: A Siike-enforced harpoon for hunting sea and other water creatures. The rope attached to the blade head is enriched with Siike-conducive properties, allowing the user to discharge Siike energy for added damage.
name: Siike Harpoon
usage:
Manual + Aim + 3d10 (if the shooter’s Athletics or Brute Force Attribute is 2 or higher, no bonus otherwise)

Siike-Powered: The Siike Harpoon has 3 Siike charges. Once the harpoon has hit its target and while the target is pierced by it, the shooter can use a Reaction to use one Siike charge to cause 1d6 additional damage. The victim can use an Action to attempt to break from the harpoon: roll Athletics OR Brute Force + Agility; the number of successes must be equal or higher than the difference between the previous Attack minus Defence roll.

The Siike Harpoon recovers all three charges after One Phase of direct exposure to the Beam OR three hours at a Siike charging point.

description: Armour is basic for those engaging in combat.
name: Armour Grade 1
usage:
Grade 1: 1d10 to Melee AND Range Defence Rolls + 5 HP.

Always protected: As stated in the combat mechanics, the modifier is added to both Melee and Range Defence Rolls as d10s; additionally, if out of Defence Reactions, the player can still roll the armour modifier. For example, if a character’s Defence Roll (Agility + Acrobatics + Armour modifier) is 7, but they don't have any Reactions left to make a Defence roll, they can still roll the Armour Modifier dice to defend: 1d10 Armour Grade 1. Even when not dodging out of the way, the armour still protects them.

Damage Absorber: The Armour HPs go first and this is damage the character doesn’t have to take.

Repairable: To recover its HP value, the Armour needs to be repaired by a professional. Failing that, a character can make a Manual + Mechanics roll and restore 1 HPs per success roll. If a character has the Techno Crafter path, they can add 1d6 HP per Success instead.

Faulty: The Armour modifier cannot be applied to Defence Rolls while the armour is at 0 HP.

Broken: If a piece of armour receives twice its HP (5 HP for Armour Grade 1) in damage in one blow, it is broken beyond repair.

description: Armour is basic for those engaging in combat. Grade 2 armour is conspicuous, making it hard for the wearer not to stand out. Additionally, non-trained wearers will move more slowly while wearing it.
name: Armour Grade 2
usage:
Grade 2: 2d10 to Melee AND Range Defence Rolls + 10 HP.

Always protected: As stated in the combat mechanics, the modifier is added to both Melee and Range Defence Rolls as d10s; additionally, if out of Defence Reactions, the player can still roll the armour modifier. For example, if a character’s Defence Roll (Agility + Acrobatics + Armour modifier) is 7, but they don't have any Reactions left to make a Defence roll, they can still roll the Armour Modifier dice to defend: 2d10 for Armour Grade 2. Even when not dodging out of the way, the armour still protects them.

Damage Absorber: The Armour HPs go first and this is damage the character doesn’t have to take.

Repairable: To recover its HP value, the Armour needs to be repaired by a professional. Failing that, a character can make a Manual + Mechanics roll and restore 1 HPs per success roll. If a character has the Techno Crafter path, they can add 1d6 HP per Success instead.

Faulty: The Armour modifier cannot be applied to Defence Rolls while the armour is at 0 HP.

Broken: If a piece of armour receives twice its HP in damage in one blow, it is broken beyond repair.

Encumbering: Grade 2 armour imposes a 1d10 penalty to Agility- and Athletic-based checks per level above a character’s Agility or Athletics Attribute (whichever is higher). For example, a character with 1 in Athletics and Agility would have a 1d10 penalty to any Athletics and Agility checks when wearing Grade 2 Armour. This includes Defence Rolls, meaning that, for some characters, wearing armour may be counterproductive.

Conspicuous: Grade 2 imposes a 2d10 penalty on Stealth-based checks.

costInfo: Includes helmet.
description:
Armour is basic for those engaging in combat.

Grade 3 armour is exceedingly conspicuous, including a helmet, which makes it impossible for the wearer not to stand out in any situation other than combat.

Additionally, non-trained wearers will struggle to move normally while wearing it.
name: Armour Grade 3
usage:
Grade 3: 3d10 to Melee AND Range Defence Rolls + 15 HP (protection against headshots).

Always protected: As stated in the combat mechanics, the modifier is added to both Melee and Range Defence Rolls as d10s; additionally, if out of Defence Reactions, the player can still roll the armour modifier. For example, if a character’s Defence Roll (Agility + Acrobatics + Armour modifier) is 7, but they don't have any Reactions left to make a Defence roll, they can still roll the Armour Modifier dice to defend: 3d10 for Armour Grade 2. Even when not dodging out of the way, the armour still protects them.

Damage Absorber: The Armour HPs go first and this is damage the character doesn’t have to take.

Repairable: To recover its HP value, the Armour needs to be repaired by a professional. Failing that, a character can make a Manual + Mechanics roll and restore 1 HPs per success roll. If a character has the Techno Crafter path, they can add 1d6 HP per Success instead.

Faulty: The Armour modifier cannot be applied to Defence Rolls while the armour is at 0 HP.

Broken: If a piece of armour receives twice its HP in damage in one blow, it is broken beyond repair.

Encumbering: Grade 3 imposes a 1d10 penalty to Agility- and Athletic-based checks per level above a character’s Agility or Athletics Attribute (whichever is higher). For example, a character with 1 in Athletics and Agility would have a 2d10 penalty to any Athletics and Agility checks when wearing Grade 3 Armour. This includes Defence Rolls, meaning that, for some characters, wearing armour may be counterproductive.

Conspicuous: Grade 3 imposes a 2d10 penalty on Stealth-based checks per Grade above 1. For instance, a Stealth check while wearing Grade 3 armour would impose a 4d10 penalty.

costInfo: Includes helmet.
description:
Armour is basic for those engaging in combat.

Grade 4 armour is exceedingly conspicuous, including a military-grade helmet, which makes it impossible for the wearer not to stand out in any situation other than combat.

Additionally, non-trained wearers will struggle to move in it.
name: Armour Grade 4
usage:
Grade 4: 4d10 to Melee AND Range Defence Rolls + 20 HP (protection against headshots).

Always protected: As stated in the combat mechanics, the modifier is added to both Melee and Range Defence Rolls as d10s; additionally, if out of Defence Reactions, the player can still roll the armour modifier. For example, if a character’s Defence Roll (Agility + Acrobatics + Armour modifier) is 7, but they don't have any Reactions left to make a Defence roll, they can still roll the Armour Modifier dice to defend: 4d10 for Armour Grade 4. Even when not dodging out of the way, the armour still protects them.

Damage Absorber: The Armour HPs go first and this is damage the character doesn’t have to take.

Repairable: To recover its HP value, the Armour needs to be repaired by a professional. Failing that, a character can make a Manual + Mechanics roll and restore 1 HPs per success roll. If a character has the Techno Crafter path, they can add 1d6 HP per Success instead.

Faulty: The Armour modifier cannot be applied to Defence Rolls while the armour is at 0 HP.

Broken: If a piece of armour receives twice its HP in damage in one blow, it is broken beyond repair.

Encumbering: Grade 4 imposes a 1d10 penalty to Agility- and Athletic-based checks per level above a character’s Agility or Athletics Attribute (whichever is higher). For example, a character with 1 in Athletics and Agility would have a 3d10 penalty if the amour were Grade 4. This includes Defence Rolls, meaning that, for some characters, wearing armour may be counterproductive.

Conspicuous: Grade 4 imposes a 2d10 penalty on Stealth-based checks per Grade above 1. For instance, a Stealth check while wearing Grade 4 armour would impose a 6d10 penalty.

costInfo: Includes helmet.
description:
Armour is basic for those engaging in combat.

Grade 5 armour is exceedingly conspicuous, including a military-grade helmet and bulky plates, which makes it impossible for the wearer not to stand out in any situation other than combat.

Additionally, non-trained wearers will struggle to move in it.
name: Armour Grade 5
usage:
Grade 5: 5d10 to Melee AND Range Defence Rolls + 25 HP (protection against headshots).

Always protected: As stated in the combat mechanics, the modifier is added to both Melee and Range Defence Rolls as d10s; additionally, if out of Defence Reactions, the player can still roll the armour modifier. For example, if a character’s Defence Roll (Agility + Acrobatics + Armour modifier) is 7, but they don't have any Reactions left to make a Defence roll, they can still roll the Armour Modifier dice to defend: 4d10 for Armour Grade 4. Even when not dodging out of the way, the armour still protects them.

Damage Absorber: The Armour HPs go first and this is damage the character doesn’t have to take.

Repairable: To recover its HP value, the Armour needs to be repaired by a professional. Failing that, a character can make a Manual + Mechanics roll and restore 1 HPs per success roll. If a character has the Techno Crafter path, they can add 1d6 HP per Success instead.

Faulty: The Armour modifier cannot be applied to Defence Rolls while the armour is at 0 HP.

Broken: If a piece of armour receives twice its HP in damage in one blow, it is broken beyond repair.

Encumbering: Grade 4 imposes a 1d10 penalty to Agility- and Athletic-based checks per level above a character’s Agility or Athletics Attribute (whichever is higher). For example, a character with 1 in Athletics and Agility would have a 4d10 penalty if the amour were Grade 4. This includes Defence Rolls, which means, for some characters, wearing armour may be counterproductive.

Conspicuous: Grade 5 imposes a 2d10 penalty on Stealth-based checks per Grade above 1. For instance, a Stealth check while wearing Grade 5 armour would impose a 8d10 penalty.

description: The mask protects against known harmful gases and spores.
name: Protection Mask (regular)
usage: While wearing the mask, Resistance (Internal) checks against noxious air have a 2d10 bonus.

description: The mask protects against known harmful gases and spores.
name: Protection Mask (professional)
usage: While wearing the mask, Resistance (Internal) checks against noxious air have a 4d10 bonus.

description: The mask offers the wearer full protection against known harmful gases and spores.
name: Protection Mask (advanced)
usage:
While wearing the mask, the wearer succeeds automatically on checks against noxious air.

However, Perception-based checks incur a minus 2d10 penalty due to the bulkiness of the mask.

description: The spores antidote cures the effects of most known harmful spores. The antidote has been designed to be administered orally or intravenously.
name: Spores Antidote
usage:
Orally: add 5d10 to a Resistance (Internal) check. On a success, the poisoning ends.
Intravenously: ad 10d10 to a Resistance (Internal) check. On a success, the poisoning ends.

description:
Designed for exploring the wilderness, this bomb combines the powers of both the anti-scent bomb and the fast-flash bomb.

An anti-scent bomb releases pheromones that mask Human, Kinian or Fenne odours. They are mostly used to confuse beasts that may be trailing them.

A fast-flash bomb releases a cloud of shimmering energy within which fast flashes of energy shoot about, throwing a beast off its quarry, unable to focus on other movement.
name: Combination Bomb
notes:
Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage: Upon impact, the bomb creates a 50-metre radius anti-scent area and a 30-metre radius fast-flash area for 5 minutes.

description: A fast-flash bomb releases a cloud of shimmering energy within which fast flashes of energy shoot about, throwing a beast off its quarry, unable to focus on other movement.
name: Fast-Flash Bomb
notes:
Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage: Upon impact, the bomb creates a 30-metre radius fast-flash area for 5 minutes.

description: A smoke bomb releases an immense cloud of grey smoke, creating an area of obscurity.
name: Smoke Bomb
notes:
Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage: Upon impact, the bomb creates a 20-metre radius obscured area for 5 minutes.

description: An anti-scent bomb releases pheromones that mask Human, Kinian or Fenne odours. They are mostly used to confuse beasts that may be trailing them.
name: Anti-Scent Bomb
notes:
Throw mechanics: Throw Attack + any modifier.

The maximum range is 10 (Fenne), 15 (Human, Manfenn), 20 (Kinian) + the thrower's Athletics + Brute Force + Manual + Agility scores. If the thrower's Path is Soldier, they can add 10 metres to the maximum range.

On a Success, the projectile explodes in the intended spot.

On a failure, roll 1d2: on a 1, the throw is short; on a 2, it's long. Next, roll 1d4 to determine direction from the thrower's perspective (1 is hard left, 2 soft left, 3 soft right, 4 hard right) and 1d4 to determine how far off (in metres) in the rolled direction the projectile goes.
usage: Upon impact, the bomb creates a 50-metre radius anti-scent area for 5 minutes.

description: A GRID-operated beacon to form security perimeters and alert the user of intruders.
name: Laser Perimeter Beacons
notes: Weight per beacon.
usage: Each beacon can shoot a laser to another to create a line. They can be up to 15 metres apart from each other. With 3, the user can create a perimeter. In either case, the beacons can be set via GRID to alert the user of an individual or object crossing the space (but the laser can be avoided if seen).

description: The security string is powered by nanobots and is used to establish practically invisible perimeters around a protected item or person.
name: Smart Perimiter String
usage:
The string is 20m long.
The user places the string (normally on the floor) around an object. They are connected via GRID. Individuals can be added to a safe list. Otherwise, when someone or something crosses it, the user will be alerted of the intrusion.

description: The smart fishing line can be thrown into a pool of water where fish live and it will scan for the right type of fish and catch them automatically. The type of fish can be specified. The user can connect to the smart fishing line via GRID to see underwater.
name: Smart Fishing Line
usage: The line is 50m long. Roll Manual/Mentality + Aim/GRID to direct the line under water.

description: A sleek boomerang used by explorers and reinforced with Siike for additional functionality and power.
name: Siike Boomerang
notes: A charge is restored per phase of direct exposure to the Beam or one hour of it being plugged to a Siike charger.
usage:
Throw Attack (Athletics + Aim + 3d10). On a hit, it deals 1d6 Bluegeoning damage. On a miss, the boomerang returns to the attacker without needing to expend a charge.

The Siike Hunter’s Boomerang has 6 charges:

- On a hit, a charge can be added to to deal 1d6 additional Siike damage (only one charge per hit to deal additional damage).

- A charge can be used to have it return to the attacker (it returns at the beginning of the attacker's next turn).

- A charge can also be used to slow the boomerang at the critical moment and deal non-lethal demage if reducing the target to 0 HP, knocking them unconscious but stable instead.

One to three charges can be used on one throw simultaneously to trigger the three effects at once.

Recover 3 charges per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

description: A boomerang used by explorers.
name: Boomerang
usage:
Throw Attack (Athletics + Aim + 2d10).

On a hit, it deals 1d6 Bluegeoning damage.

On a miss, the boomerang returns to the attacker.

description: The software allows the user to perform GRID attacks.
name: GRID Attack Patch Grade 1
notes:
Acquiring a legal patch requires the user to be registered in the Confederal Advanced GRID Users Database (CAGUD).

Illegal patches exist but they incur heavy punishments. Additionally, they can mess with the user’s GRID, causing damage on faulty installations at the GM's discretion.
usage:
Before a character can launch a GRID Attack, they need to connect with the target’s GRID. It takes one Action to break into the target’s GRID.

Breaking into target’s GRID: Mentality + GRID + 1d10 (grade 1 patch).

Once broken into a target’s GRID, an Action can be used to do a GRID Attack while the target is in range.

Attacking: Mentality + GRID + 1d10 (grade 1 patch). On a hit, the attacker deals GRID-induced Physical Damage.

description: The software allows the user to perform more powerful GRID attacks from a greater distance.
name: GRID Attack Patch Grade 2
notes:
Acquiring a legal patch requires the user to be registered in the Confederal Advanced GRID Users Database (CAGUD).

Illegal patches exist but they incur heavy punishments. Additionally, they can mess with the user’s GRID, causing damage on faulty installations at the GM's discretion.
usage:
Before a character can launch a GRID Attack, they need to connect with the target’s GRID. It takes one Action to break into the target’s GRID.

Breaking into target’s GRID: Mentality + GRID + 2d10 (grade 2 attack patch).

Once broken into a target’s GRID, an Action can be used to do a GRID Attack while the target is in range.

Attacking: Mentality + GRID + 2d10 (grade 2 attack patch). On a hit, the attacker deals GRID-induced Physical Damage.

description: The software allows the user to perform exceedingly powerful GRID attacks from a significant distance, causing physical or mental damage at will.
name: GRID Attack Patch Grade 3
notes:
Acquiring a legal patch requires the user to be registered in the Confederal Advanced GRID Users Database (CAGUD).

Illegal patches exist but they incur heavy punishments. Additionally, they can mess with the user’s GRID, causing damage on faulty installations at the GM's discretion.
usage:
The user can break into the target's GRID or choose to Attack as part of the breaking into the target's GRID.

Breaking and/or Attacking into target’s GRID: Mentality + GRID + 3d10 (grade 3 attack patch).

On a hit, the attacker deals GRID-induced Physical OR Mental Damage, as chosen by the attacker.

description: The software provides the user with additional protection and updated safety protocols to defend against GRID attacks.
name: GRID Defence Patch Grade 1
notes: Illegal patches exist but they incur heavy punishments. Additionally, they can mess with the user’s GRID, causing damage on faulty installations at the GM's discretion.
usage: GRID Defence roll: Mentality + GRID + 1d10 (grade 1 defence patch).

description: The software provides the user with additional protection and updated safety protocols to defend against GRID attacks.
name: GRID Defence Patch Grade 2
notes: Illegal patches exist but they incur heavy punishments. Additionally, they can mess with the user’s GRID, causing damage on faulty installations at the GM's discretion.
usage: GRID Defence roll: Mentality + GRID + 2d10 (grade 2 defence patch).

description: The software provides the user with additional protection and updated safety protocols to defend against GRID attacks.
name: GRID Defence Patch Grade 3
notes: Illegal patches exist but they incur heavy punishments. Additionally, they can mess with the user’s GRID, causing damage on faulty installations at the GM's discretion.
usage: GRID Defence roll: Mentality + GRID + 3d10 (grade 3 defence patch).

description: The Siike boots are imbued with Siike energy that soften the pressure on the wearer’s body, thus reducing the physical toll and allowing the wearer to walk in them for longer without incurring exhaustion.
name: Siike Trekking Boots
usage:
The Siike boots have 10 charges. Every hour of use (when activated) expends one charge. Each charge allows the wearer to cover twice as much distance as they normally would at their normal pace without having to make Stamina + Resilience rolls.

The wearer still needs the habitual one phase of rest per day to avoid becoming exhausted.

The 10 charges are recovered in one phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or one hour in a charging point. A portable Siike charger can add 5 charges in 2 hours.

description: The augmented vision goggles allow the user to see in the dark to a longer range than when simply using a GRID patch. They also allow the user to see at twice the distance they normally would.
name: Augmented Vision Goggles (regular)
usage: The goggles allow the wearer to see in the dark for up to 100 metres, and normally for twice the distance they normally would.

description: The advanced augmented vision goggles allow the user to see in the dark to a longer range than when simply using a GRID patch. They also allow the user to see at twice the distance they normally would. Additionally, they detect fast movement otherwise imperceptible, as well as movement detection on the user's blindspots. Finally, they also provide heat source detection.
name: Augmented Vision Goggles (advanced)
usage:
The goggles allow the wearer to see in the dark for up to 100 metres, and normally for twice the distance they normally would.

The user can spot ultra fast or slow movement.

The user is alerted about movement in their blindspots.

The user can detect heat sources.

description: A rifle that shoots bullets at great distances with accuracy, dealing devastating damage.
name: Common Rifle
notes: Common Rifles are becoming less and less common in the early 2100s as their Siike counterparts take over both in law and order and the criminal world.
usage:
For range attacks: 3d10 bonus to attack. On a hit, the target takes 3d10 Bullet damage.

Every 10m beyond the weapon's range incurs a -1d10 penalty (minimum 1d10). At 50% over the range, the weapon does not reach its target, regardless of the Attack (Range) roll result.

Every 10m beyond the weapon's range incurs a -1d10 penalty (minimum 1d10). At 50% over the range, the weapon does not reach its target, regardless of the Attack (Range) roll result.

description: A gun shoots bullets at great distances with accuracy, dealing devastating damage.
name: Common Gun
notes: Common Guns are becoming less and less common in the early 2100s as their Siike counterparts take over both in law and order and the criminal world.
usage:
For range attacks: 2d10 bonus to attack. On a hit, the target takes 2d10 Bullet damage.

Every 10m beyond the weapon's range incurs a -1d10 penalty (minimum 1d10). At 50% over the range, the weapon does not reach its target, regardless of the Attack (Range) roll result.

Every 10m beyond the weapon's range incurs a -1d10 penalty (minimum 1d10). At 50% over the range, the weapon does not reach its target, regardless of the Attack (Range) roll result.

costInfo: 5 uses, shots available for 200 each.
description: The shot pumps renewed life on a hurt individual but it does so by hijacking the brain’s normal functioning.
name: Adrenaline Shot Injector
notes:
Regular use of the product will cause addiction.

The GM may ask the player to make a Resistance (Internal) every time they use an adrenaline shot once addicted; on three failures, the character incurs a Serious Mental Trauma, and must continue to use the Adrenaline Shot once a day.
usage:
A character can use their Action or Reaction to inject themselves or their Action to inject somebody else.

The Adrenaline Shot gives the taker 1d6 Physical HP and subtracts the same amount from their Mental HP.

description: A container of concentrated Siike energy to be used on devices that feed on it.
name: Portable Siike Charger (C1)
usage:
Plug the charger to a Siike-powered device to regain one charge.

The charger regains 1 charge per phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or 1 per hour when plugged to a direct Siike point.

description: A container of concentrated Siike energy to be used on devices that feed on it.
name: Portable Siike Charger (C5)
usage:
Plug the charger to a Siike-powered device to regain the desired number of charges.

The charger regains 1 charge per phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or 1 per hour when plugged to a direct Siike point.

description: A container of concentrated Siike energy to be used on devices that feed on it.
name: Portable Siike Charger (C10)
usage:
Plug the charger to a Siike-powered device to regain the desired number of charges.

The charger regains 1 charge per phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or 1 per hour when plugged to a direct Siike point.

description: A container of concentrated Siike energy to be used on devices that feed on it.
name: Portable Siike Charger (C15)
usage:
Plug the charger to a Siike-powered device to regain the desired number of charges.

The charger regains 1 charge per phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or 1 per hour when plugged to a direct Siike point.

description: A container of concentrated Siike energy to be used on devices that feed on it.
name: Portable Siike Charger (C20)
usage:
Plug the charger to a Siike-powered device to regain the desired number of charges.

The charger regains 1 charge per phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or 1 per hour when plugged to a direct Siike point.

description: The Water Detection Bracelet connects with the user’s GRID via a software patch. It can read the air for signs of water sources. It checks humidity levels and uses the readings to pin-point the approximate direction of the nearest source of water.
name: Water Detection Bracelet
usage: Hold the bracelet in the air. The GRID overlay will point the most likely direction of the nearest source of water within a 500-metre radius. The bracelet provides information as to the probable nature of the source and the quantity and quality of its water. It also shows the accuracy of the detection in percentage points.

description: The Water Detection device can read the air for signs of water sources. It checks humidity levels and uses the readings to pin-point the approximate direction of the nearest source of water.
name: Water Detection Device
usage: Hold the device in the air. The screen will point the most likely direction of the nearest source of water within a 1-kilometre radius. The device provides information as to the probable nature of the source and the quantity and quality of its water. It also shows the accuracy of the detection in percentage points.

description: One daily nourishment pill provides the taker with one-day worth of nutrients. It doesn’t provide hydration.
name: Daily Nourishment Pills
notes: One bottle contains 20 pills.
usage: Take one pill and have enough nourishment for a day. It gives the taker enough sustenance for two phases.

description: The Siike stove allows a character to cook in the wild.
name: Siike Portable Stove
usage: The portable Siike stove allows for 10 hours (1 per charge) of interrupted cooking. It can be charged on a Siike charging point (regains all 10 charges in one phase) or with a portable Siike charger (one charge per two hours).

description: One tablet purifies a litre of water.
name: Water Purification Tablets
notes: The bottle contains 100 tablets.
usage: Throw the tablet in a container containing a litre of water and the water will be safe to drink in 5 minutes.

description: One Full-Meal pill provides the consumer with all the nourishment necessary for one meal. It does not provide hydration.
name: Full-Meal Pills
notes: A bottle contains 30 pills.
usage: A character eats one pill and doesn’t need to eat again for up to 8 hours. Two pills are enough sustenance for a day.

costInfo: The cost is only indicative, based on black market transactions. Not for sale. Chosen Faith Kinians receive one of these at their Embracing when they come of age.
description: The life-saving amulet is programmed to detect potential loss of Kinian life (through the individual’s Siike connection) and shock vital energy into the body, restoring the wearer to an unconscious but stable condition.
name: Life-Saving Chosen Amulet
notes: This esoteric function is often seen in Chosen Faith's Embracing (“coming of age”) amulets. Only a handful of scientists in Neblir are capable of concentrating Siike to the degree required and programme its release to be linked to the Siike energy of the wearer and detect when life is in danger.
usage:
The amulet must be worn or held by a Kinian. It must be in contact with Kinian skin for it to have the desired effect.

If the Kinian wearing the amulet drops to 0 HP, they don’t do death saves. They are unconscious but stable.

If the Siike energy in the amulet has been “tied” to the blood of the Kinian individual in particular, upon dropping to 0HP, the wearer bounces back to consciousness, restoring 1d6 Physical HP per character level.

The amulet only works once. Once used, it is partially broken and charred.

description: The throwable hammer has a display that shoots a laser at a target, locks it, then the user can throw it there with precision. The hammer detaches from its handle when thrown and then returns to it after hitting its target.
name: Throwable Hammer
usage:
At close distance, make a Melee Attack with the hammer. On a hit, it deals 1d6 Bludgeoning damage.

At 5 metres or more, make a Throw Attack to throw the hammer. On a success, you lock the target and hit it. If used to attack an enemy, on a hit, it deals 2d6 Bluegeoning damage. On a failure, it hits a point 1d4 metres off target in a 1d4 direction (1 is hard left from the character's perspective).

At the start of the attacker's next turn, when the hammer returns, the user must use a Reaction to make Manual + Athletics check. On a success, the hammer attaches itself back to the handle; on a failure, make an free (part of the same Reaction) Agility + Acrobatics check to avoid being hit or take 1d6 damage. The hammer head falls 1d4 metres behind the character in a 1d4 direction (1 hard left from the character's perspective).

description: The portable lab allows the user to perform chemistry at an advanced level while on the go. It might not allow them to perform large-scale operations, but it is more than enough for high-quality experimenting and testing, one test at a time.
name: Portable Lab
usage:
You may perform one operation at a time in the portable lab.

It has 20 charges, using 1 per hour of operation. It recovers one charge per phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or one per hour if plugged to a Siike source. It can work without expending charges if plugged to a Siike source.

It can be left to work alone on a programmed cycle.

description: The Grappling Hook shoots to a point up to 50m. Its hook, made of AI powered nano pointers, can attach itself securely to all manner of surfaces, from metal to rock to synthetic. As long as the surface is solid and secure enough to hold the shooter, the shooter can press a button to be propelled towards the hook. Alternatively, the shooter can release the hook, which will fly back to the thrower.
name: Grappling Hook
usage:
It takes one Action to shoot the hook: Manual +  Aim + 2d10.

It takes another Action to Make an Athletics + Agility check to fly towards the hook. On a failure, if in Initiative, the character loses all Reactions for that turn, or if they've already used any, they lose grasp of the grappling hook and fall to the ground mid pull, taking damage at the GM's discretion.

Alternatively, it takes also one Action (but no roll) to release the hook and have it fly back to the user.

description: A custom-made foldable knife, especifically desgined for a two-thumbed Fenne hand. The sleekness of its design makes it more lethal than a common knife.
name: Fenne Foldable Knife
usage: Make a Melee Attack + 3d10, dealing 2d6 Blade damage.

costInfo:
One item of ammunition costs 1000 CONF.

Custom made for Dr Orega Plynth via Tanit Fuz (actual maker Prilla Ont).
description: A strange bazooka that shoots a storm of blades around its target. Dr Orega Plynth commissioned one as last resort measure to contain some of her bilogical experimental creatures, which were known to be vulnerable to blade damage and resistant to Siike.
name: Plynth's Blade Bazooka
notes: Every 10 metres above the weapon’s range incurs a 1-die penalty on the attack roll.
usage:
It takes one Action to reload the bazooka, which can hold 1 explosive at a time.

Make a Range Attack + 5d10.

Subtract 2d10 from the Attack roll for every point below 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 1 in Athletics and 3 in Brute Force would have a 4d10 penalty on the Range Attack)

On a hit, a blast of blades radiates from from the desired spot, dealing 10d6 Siike damage around a 5-metre radius from the exploding point, 5d6 within a 10-metre radius.

On a failure, the blast explodes 1d10 metres off target in a 1d8 direction from the intended target from the shooter's perspective (1 is hard left, 3 is ahead, 4 soft right and ahead, 5 is hard right, 7 is short, 8 is soft left and short, etc.).

Dodging the Blade Bazooka: one Reaction to make a Perception + Mentality to spot where it’s going and have the sangfroid to react by jumping, then if successful, another Reaction to make an Agility + Acrobatics to move 2 metres per Success away from the blast point, then a free (part of the previous Reaction) Resistance (External) + Resilience to remove 1d6 per Success from the damage dealt.

The Bazooka has 2 uses.

costInfo:
10 razor blooming bullets cost 500 CONF.

Custom made for Dr Orega Plynth via Tanit Fuz (actual maker Prilla Ont)
description: A strange bazooka that shoots a bullets that bloom as razors upon impact. Dr Orega Plynth commissioned one as last resort measure to contain some of her bilogical experimental creatures, which were known to be vulnerable to blade damage and resistant to Siike.
name: Plynth's Razor Rifle
notes:
It only affects flesh. It has no effect on armour.

The razor-bullet opens and spins, mincing the area it hits.

Every 10 metres above the weapon’s range incurs a 1-die penalty on the attack roll.
usage:
The rifle holds 10 razor blooming bullets.

Subtract 1d10 from the Attack roll for every point below 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 1 in Athletics and 3 in Brute Force would have a 2d10 penalty on the Range Attack)

The Razor Rifle has 10 uses.

costInfo: One item of ammunition costs 500 CONF.
description: A bazooka that shoots a Siike explosive that blasts around its target, causing devastating damage.
name: Siike Bazooka
notes: Every 10 metres above the weapon’s range incurs a 1-die penalty on the attack roll.
usage:
It takes one Action to reload the bazooka, which can hold 1 explosive at a time.

Make a Range Attack + 5d10.

Subtract 2d10 from the Attack roll for every point below 3 in any of the shooter’s Athletics AND Brute Force Attributes (e.g. a character with 1 in Athletics and 3 in Brute Force would have a 4d10 penalty on the Range Attack)

On a hit, a Siike blast emanates from from the desired spot, dealing 20d6 Siike damage around a 5-metre radius from the exploding point, 15d6 around a 10-metre radius from the blast point, 10d6 within a 15-metre radius, and 5d6 within a 20-metre radius.

On a failure, the blast explodes 1d10 metres off target in a 1d8 direction from the intended target from the shooter's perspective (1 is hard left, 3 is ahead, 4 soft right and ahead, 5 is hard right, 7 is short, 8 is soft left and short, etc.).

Dodging the Siike bazooka: one Reaction to make a Perception + Mentality to spot where it’s going and have the sangfroid to react by jumping, then if successful, another Reaction to make an Agility + Acrobatics to move 2 metres per Success away from the blast point, then a free (part of the previous Reaction) Resistance (External) + Resilience to remove 1d6 per Success from the damage dealt.

The Siike Bazooka has 3 charges. It recovers one per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

costInfo: Custom item, bespoke, by commission only, price may fluctuate.
description: The foldable Siike turret is a portable defensive device that can be easily attached to any surface. It connects to the user’s GRID and alerts them when it detects movements in the previously set perimeter. The user can see through the turret. The user can then command them to shoot. They can also be set to shoot automatically upon detection of an intruder.
name: Prilla Ont's Portable Turret
notes: Originally devised by Prilla Ont.
usage:
The user needs to connect to the turret via GRID to programme it.

If set to manual mode, the user can make Perception + Investigation checks to see intruders through the turret, directly from the user's GRID overlay. In manual mode, the user makes uses an Action to make a Mentality + Aim + 5d10 check to shoot from the turret; a user can control a number of turrets equal to their Mentality + Aim score, allowing the user to shoot multiple turrets with one action, dividing the score among the turrets (for instance, if the user has 8d10 for the Mentality + Aim check, it can shoot 8 turrets with one Action, each with 6d10 (including the turret's bonus), 4 with 7d10 (including the turret's bonus), 2 with 9d10 (including the turret's bonus), etc.

If set to automatic mode, the user can programme the turret to shoot upon detection of movement or GRID signal, or both. The user can also exclude specific GRID signatures from the target list, or programme the turret only to shoot against specific GRID signatures. In automatic mode, the turret shoots with 10d10.

If the target hasn't noticed the turret's presence, they cannot defend (although armour will allow them to roll against the attack). If they notice the turret, they defend normally as they would against a Range Attack. If they notice the turret before entering its range, they can make a Stealth + Surival against 10d10 not to trigger it.

The turret's range is a 30m cone from its location in the direction it points. The turret can move its cone. A sweeping movement can be programmed too.

On a hit, the turret deals 2d6 Siike damage.

The turret has 10 uses. Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

costInfo: Custom item, bespoke, by commission only, price may fluctuate.
description: Prilla Ont’s right leg is a cyborg leg devised by her when she lost her natural leg in a car accident in the Buffer. She powered it with Siike to gain additional bonuses.
name: Prilla Ont's Cyborg Leg
notes: Originally devised by Prilla Ont.
usage:
The user's Agility attribute is 5 as long as the prosthetic leg is on and operational.

The user can jump 3 times as high as normal (Athletics + Acrobatics * 3) and run 50% faster than the user's natural speed.

After 3 consecutive jumps, the leg overheats (equivalent to jumping on every turn for three turns), and jumping is reduced to half the user's natural ability; the user's speed is also reduced to half the natural score. Additionally, the user's Agility attribute is lowered to 1 while the leg is in this state. 

The user can sprint for longer than most people (10 turns in a chase), but if the user reaches those 10 consecutive turns, the cyborg leg locks up and they can’t run: the user's speed is reduced to half their natural score; jumping is also reduced to half their natural ability. Additionally, the user's Agility attribute is lowered to 1 while the leg is in this state.

Whenever the overheating or locking-up condition occure, roll 1d100: on a 5 or lower, the prosthetic leg breaks, making the condition permanent. If it doesn't break but the condition is incurred again before it can be checked by a professional, the breakage threshold is doubled (the doubling happens each time, i.e. first to 10, then 20, etc.).

The leg has 15 HP. Attacks targeted at it have a minus 4d10 penalty, but deal double damage if using Siike or Electric damage. To regain HP, the leg must be detached and repaired by a professional (Manual + Mechanics, 1 HP recovered per Success). At 0 HP, the leg is broken and needs replacing entirely.

costInfo: Custom item, bespoke, by commission only, price may fluctuate.
description: Prilla Ont’s cyborg arm is incredibly mobile and dexterous. The hand boasts the two Fenne-like thumbs; each finger has a set of drivers and scalpel-like cutting utensils that can be turned on and off via GRID. Additionally, it can be used to shoot Siike with great precision.
name: Prilla Ont's Cyborg Arm
notes: Originally devised by Prilla Ont.
usage:
The user's Manual attribute is 5 as long as the prosthetic arm is on and operational.

Any checks involving Mechanics gain a 2d10 bonus.

The prosthetic arm can hold 5 Siike charges, offers a 5d10 to hit bonus on Range and Melee Attacks while it has charges, and it deals 2d6 Siike damage.

When the arm is out of Siike charges, the user's Manual attribute is 1, and any Mechanics checks have a minus 4d10 penalty.

The arm has 10 HP. Attacks targeted at it have a minus 4d10 penalty, but deal double damage if using Siike or Electric damage. To regain HP, the arm must be detached and repaired by a professional (Manual + Mechanics, 1 HP recovered per Success). At 0 HP, the arm is broken and needs replacing entirely.

The arm has 20 Siike charges. Recover 3 per Phase of direct exposure to the Beam, or per 1 hour of being plugged to a Siike charging point.

costInfo: Custom item, bespoke, by commission only, price may fluctuate. Unless the natural eye is provided, the price might be twice the stated one.
description: Prilla Ont lost her eye in the same car accident that claimed her leg/ She created a cyborg counterpart eye after buying a Fenne eye in the black market, mixing it with tech of her own making. She mostly managed to recreate all the functionality a Fenne eye would naturally have, but more recently, she added additional features: she can see in complete darkness and up to five times farther than a normal eye. It can also be used as a microscope, which is exceedingly useful when working on manual and mechanical crafts.
name: Prilla Ont's Cyborg Eye
notes: Originally devised by Prilla Ont.
usage:
The eye grants the user a 2d10 bonus in any Investigation, Perception and Aim (including Range Attacks) checks (add the bonus once only if it's an Investigation + Perception check), and 4d10 on Mechanics checks that require a close look into small mechanical parts.

Additionally, the user can take 1 Action to discover the score of one Attribute or Skill from a character they can see.

However, the user is vulnerable to GRID Attacks, which, when they hit, deal double damage.

description: A helmet device to turn water into breathable air with settings for optimal air composition for Kinian, Human, Fenne and Manfenn .
name: Water Breathing Helmet
usage:
The helmet has 3 charges, each of which grants an hour of breathing underwater.

A charge is recovered by direct exposure to the Beam (cannot be underwater) for one Phase, or per hour if plugged to a Siike point.

costInfo: This is an average price tag for a shoebox-sized box. There is a price range for secure boxes depending on their size, and even levels of security.
description:
Secure Boxes are used to store items safely by linking the box's locking mechanism to the user's GRID. Breaking into them is near-impossible and only achievable by GRID experts.

Depending on the size and the level of security required, the box may go up or down in price at the GM's discretion.
name: Secure Box
usage:
Make a Mentality + GRID check to lock the Secure Box to a character's GRID. On a failure, the box still locks but any success on a GRID Attack will open it. On a Success, add any Successes as levels of security + 1 on the box.

A Secure Box of a higher security level automatically locks to a higher level of security.

The level of security sets the number of Successes necessary to open the box.

A GRID Attack that gets enough successes breaks the locking mechanism. Alternatively, a Deduction + Software may be rolled to attempt to open the box without harming the GRID lock mechanism (the successes are once more equal to the box's levels of security).

costInfo: Blackmarket item, price might fluctuate.
description: The Searing Lantern is a powerful illumination device with a twist: at closer range, it can emit a searing beam of scorching power that deals fire damage on anyone unprotected.
name: Searing Lantern
notes: Found in underworld blackmarkets, used by alternative scientists to control some of their experiments, and in potential military operations by the Confederal Armed Forces, but this hasn't been proven.
usage:
The Searing Latern can be used as a normal flashlight with a maximum range of 200 metres that can be adjusted. A retractable hood helps keep the light hidden from certain angles so as not to give one's position away.

As a weapon, make a Range Attack as you move the lantern's beam to your target and press the damage button. On a hit, it deals 3d6 fire damage on exposed skin, or 2d6 if the skin is protected by normal clothing, and only 1d6 if the clothing is particularly protective (any armour level would count as protective, but also other materials such as Siike Overalls or synthetic garments).

The Searing Lantern in weapon mode will set any flammable materials on fire.

costInfo: Price per packet of 10. It may be cheaper, but less safe, in the blackmarket.
description: A drug devised to enhance the taker's ability to focus their attention on a subject.
name: HyperFocus
usage:
One pill gives the taker 2d10 on any Investigation-based roll requiring attention to detail for 1 Phase: this could be a crime scene if enough time is given, or a scientific paper. However, any Perception-based check is at minus 2d10 penalty.

The effects can be doubled by taking two pills at once, but the character won't be able to sleep for a day, incurring a level of Exhaustion; additionally, a Resistance (Internal) is required, and the character takes 1d6 Mental damage on a failure.

Addictive: taking one pill every day for three or more days or taking two or more pills on the same day causes addiction. Addiction forces the user to take one pill per day or suffer three days of all Investigation- AND Perception-based rolls with minus 2d10 penalty, after which, the user is clean. After 5 consecutive days of usage, the process to clean up takes 5 days. After 6 days, the addiction requires professional help at the GM's discretion.

costInfo: Price per packet of 10. It may be cheaper, but less safe, in the blackmarket.
description: A drug devised to enhance the taker's ability to perceive changes in their surroundings.
name: HyperClarity
usage:
One pill gives the taker 2d10 on any Perception-based rolls for 1 Phase: it could be keeping an eye out for suspicious movements, or trying to observe a change in how a person behaves or talks when they lie. However, Investigation-based checks are a minus 2d10 penalty for the duration.

The effects can be doubled by taking two pills at once, but the character won't be able to sleep for a day, incurring a level of Exhaustion; additionally, a Resistance (Internal) is required, and the character takes 1d6 Mental damage on a failure.

Addictive: taking one pill every day for three or more days or taking two or more pills on the same day causes addiction. Addiction forces the user to take one pill per day or suffer three days of all Perception- AND Investigation-based rolls with minus 2d10 penalty, after which, the user is clean. After 5 consecutive days of usage, the process to clean up takes 5 days. After 6 days, the addiction requires professional help at the GM's discretion.

description: A small backpack to help you carry more of the stuff the GRID can't do for you.
name: Backpack (small)
usage: Equip the backpack. Your maxCarryWeight increases by 10%.

description: A medium backpack to help you carry more of the stuff the GRID can't do for you.
name: Backpack (medium)
usage: Equip the backpack. Your maxCarryWeight increases by 20%.

description: A large backpack to help you carry more of the stuff the GRID can't do for you.
name: Backpack (large)
usage: Equip the backpack. Your maxCarryWeight increases by 30%.

description: A military-grade sniper rifle for those special operations.
name: Sniper Rifle
notes: Military use only.
usage:
The sniper rifle must be planted on its tripod to gain the range bonus. If used as a normal range weapon, it gives the user 1d10 plus to range attacks MINUS any values below 3 in Athletics.

Once installed (1 Action), the user decides on a body part and aims the rifle there (1 Action) and executes the shot making an Attack Roll (Range) plus the 5d10 bonus (another action). If targetting a non-lethal body part (e.g. a limb), the target may simply lose that part rather than die from the rolled damage if the damage would push them over 0.

A head shot that reduces the target to 0 HP causes instant death.

description: Software enhancement of aim with ranged weapons.
name: Aim Assist GRID Patch Grade 1
usage: Once installed and equipped, the Aim GRID patch enhances the ability of the user to perform Range Attacks: add the item's modifier to any check involving the Aim skill.

description: Software enhancement of aim with ranged weapons.
name: Aim Assist GRID Patch Grade 2
usage: Once installed and equipped, the Aim GRID patch enhances the ability of the user to perform Range Attacks: add the item's modifier to any check involving the Aim skill.

description: Software enhancement of aim with ranged weapons.
name: Aim Assist GRID Patch Grade 3
usage: Once installed and equipped, the Aim GRID patch enhances the ability of the user to perform Range Attacks: add the item's modifier to any check involving the Aim skill.

description: Software enhancement for all senses.
name: Perception Enhancer GRID Patch
usage: Once installed and equipped, the Perception Enhancer GRID Patch enhances the user's ability to perceive reality. Add the patch's modifier to any Perception-based rolls.

description: The Helmet Armour is essential for those exposed to highly dangerous situations, such riot police or armed forces personnel.
name: Protective Helmet
usage: If equipped, the helmet gives an additional d10 to Defence rolls and, crucially, protects the wearer against lethal headshots such as those of a Sniper Rifle (if the wearer is hit by a headshot that reduces them to 0 HP, they don't suffer an instant death but can do death rolls as normal). Additionally, the helmet makes the wearer easy to spot and stand out in a crowd. However, its many detectors and cameras alert the wearer of incoming danger.

costInfo: Lighter, smaller or more powerful versions may exist for higher prices.
description: A device that captures audio from the setting in which it has been placed and activated.
name: Spying Bug (audio only)
notes: *Some more expensive models may allow for higher storage.
usage:
Plant the bug in a location. It captures audio clearly around a 20m radius, and less clearly for every additional 10m up to a total of a 50m radius, around which the sound is barely intelligible.

To GRID or not to GRID

Connect to it via GRID to receive audio live, let it record for up to 5 hours*, or both. If the bug is discovered while connected to the GRID, it may be possible to trace the connection back to the user.

costInfo: Lighter, smaller or more powerful versions may exist for higher prices.
description: A device that captures video from the setting in which it has been placed and activated.
name: Spying Bug (video)
notes: *More expensive models may allow for more storage and range.
usage:
Lighter, smaller or more powerful versions may exist for higher prices.

Plant the bug in a location. It captures video clearly in a 10m cone, and less clearly for every additional 10m up to a total of a 50m cone, the video becoming increasingly blurry.

To GRID or not to GRID

Connect to it via GRID to receive video live, let it record for up to 2 hours*, or both. If the bug is discovered while connected to the GRID, it may be possible to trace the connection back to the user.

costInfo: Lighter, smaller or more powerful versions may exist for higher prices.
description: A device that captures audio and video from the setting in which it has been placed and activated.
name: Spying bug (audio and video)
notes: *More expensive models may allow for more storage.
usage:
Plant the bug in a location. It captures audio clearly around a 20m radius, and less clearly for every additional 10m up to a total of a 50m radius, at which the sound is barely intelligible, and video in a 10m cone, and less clearly for every additional 10m up to a total of a 50m cone, the video becoming increasingly blurry.

To GRID or not to GRID

Connect to it via GRID to receive audio live, let it record for up to 5 hours*, or both. If the bug is discovered while connected to the GRID, it may be possible to trace the connection back to the user.

costInfo: The GRID Blaster is not for sale, its legal status as debated as its existence.
description: The GRID Blaster emits a GRID-crippling wave that shatters anyone with connected to a vulnerable GRID while active.
name: GRID Blaster
notes: The GRID Blaster is a military-grade weapon developed in secret by the Second Confederation. There are rumours that the NF has also developed similar technology.
usage:
One Action to activate and throw the GRID Blaster. If activating the GRID Blaster and leaving it in place, only 1 Reaction is needed.

The GRID Blaster goes off at the start of the activating character's turn. Any GRID-chipped characters in a 20-metre radius must use a Reaction to make a GRID Defence roll; any successes on this roll subtract 1d6 from the damage roll (each character rolls 1d6 for every success, subtracting the total from the GRID Blaster's damage). The GRID Blaster deals 3d6 when set to standard damage*.

*A factory-reset GRID Blaster is fully charged for standard damage. To modify the GRID Blaster to deal maximum damage, a character must succeed in a Deduction + GRID roll, adding 1d6 damage per success, up to a maximum of 6d6. If modified, the GRID Blaster becomes unstable: if unstable, every time it is used, the GRID Blaster breaks on a 1d100 roll below 20.

After each use, the GRID Blaster needs to recharge for three Phases of direct exposure to the Beam (or one Phase if plugged to a Siike charger) and be reset with a successful Deduction + GRID roll (1 success needed).

costInfo: Prices may vary massively, as this is an illegal item and not standarised.
description: A makeshift gun from the depths of the Underworld crafted to be installed on the handle of a motorbike.
name: Bike Blaster
notes: Illegal, makeshift item, may be more or less powerful and expensive (at GM's discretion). If carried in hand, it can be shot, but both hands must be used for this, and the shooter must use 1 Reaction in addition to their Action.
usage:
If using an Action to drive, the driver can use 1 Reaction to aim and 1 Reaction to shoot. Alternatively, if forgoing the Action and letting the vehicle drive in the current direction, the driver may use their Action to shoot and keep their Reactions (including to dodge any road obstacles).

The Bike Blaster has a 2d10 bonus to Range Attacks and deals 2d6 damage.

costInfo: Mostly an illegal item, although collector pieces are legal.
description: A modified old-school shotgun for quick fire and fast intimidation. Although not the most accurate weapon, the blast of the sawed shotgun is brutal.
name: Sawed Shotgun
usage:
Make a Range Attack with 3d10 bonus if within 20m (no bonus from 20m to 60m).

On a hit, deal 3d6 Bullet damage if the target is within 20m, 2d6 Bullet damage if the target is within 40m, and 1d6 Bullet damage if the target is beyond 40m.
